  • Support review and execution of private placement new issue transactions with a focus on fundamental credit and relative value analysis
  • Provide support in secondary trading execution of private placement bonds and analysis related to market liquidity, pricing, and primary supply activity
  • Maintain and develop surveillance materials related to originations, market activity, performance monitoring, risk exposures, valuations, among other topics
  • Assist analysis and evaluation of transaction amendments and waivers by developing an understanding of covenants and transaction structures
  • Work with primary credit research analysts to monitor individual and sector credit positions
  • Work with senior team members to diligence private equity and alternative investment opportunities
  • Develop and maintain business relationships with sell-side counterparts including all sales, trading, and syndicate functions
  • Oversee monthly pricing process for private placement bond holdings in accordance to internal policies and procedures
  • Conduct and oversee ad hoc analysis to support PM, management, client, and desk-directed inquiries

Qualifications

  • 4-5 years of experience in asset management, investment management or investment banking

  • BA/BS degree

Preferred Qualifications:

  • CFA or interest in pursuing CFA designation
  • Asset Management experience: infrastructure, real assets exposure or experience
  • Strong credit, finance, economic, business, analytical, and technical skills
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, attention to detail, and problem-solving ability
  • Ability to multi-task across analytical, administrative, and research activities in a detail oriented and timely manner
  • Advanced Excel proficiency
  • Strong commitment to performance and accuracy of work product
  • Team oriented individual with ability to work well independently and with diverse and geographically dispersed groups

We are not seeking agency assistance for this role

$135,000.00-$150,000.00

jobs.title: Senior Associate - Transaction Advisory Services
jobs.description:
Description

Senior Associate - Transaction Advisory Services
Chicago, Illinois – Remote
What to expect when you join Sikich
Team members at Sikich have a lot in common while also being part of a rich and varied group of contributors, creating a distinct and thriving culture. Chief among our commonalities is a desire for growth and a shared unity of purpose in our professional lives. We believe that through varied perspectives, challenging the status quo and rewarding action, we accelerate innovation and drive growth – for our clients, for ourselves and for our communities.
The professional services landscape continues to evolve. For Sikich, this means we have an opportunity to further cement our leadership position in this industry and continue to grow our organization in increasingly exciting ways. This growth is meaningful for every team member at our company because larger companies simply see more interesting client opportunities and can attract impressively talented individuals like you. Through a dedicated focus on key business priorities and intentionally creating a rewarding employee experience, Sikich has developed into a highly regarded provider of professional services and a sought-after employer of choice.
Do you want to work with other skilled practitioners and serve clients in a way that makes a difference? Are you seeking a supportive environment backed by a deep and extensive set of skillsets? Are you ready to make an impact and be acknowledged for your contributions? If you answered yes to these questions, we see a mutually beneficial and gratifying relationship on the horizon!
Are you ready to grow with us?
Position Summary
Reporting to the TAS Manager, the Senior Associate will,
Responsibilities  
  • Perform (as part of a team) financial due diligence for clients acquiring businesses
  • Identify & analyze business & financial risks & opportunities while advising both strategic & private equity buyers & sellers on alternative strategies for acquisition, disposition, & restructuring of businesses
  • Aggregate, manipulate, & analyze target company data to identify historical & projected financial & operating trends, quality of earnings, & working capital considerations
  • Work on multiple projects of varying size & complexity
  • Participate in client meetings & site visits while interacting with top-level management to obtain pertinent information for financial analysis
  • Travel as needed to various client sites, domestic or international
Requirements  
  • Bachelor’s degree (Accounting) or equivalent experience 
  • 4+ years’ public accounting experience, preferably within a transaction advisory role,CPApreferred
  • Exceptional professional communication skills, both verbal and written, with clients including C-level executives 
  • Strong current knowledge of U.S. GAAP, familiarity of the M&A process and market, prior experience or familiarity in transaction advisory, private equity, or investment banking, and strong project management skills 
Preferred Skills
  • Active CPA certification or in the process of obtaining one
  • Excellent interpersonal and communication abilities
  • Strong project management capabilities
  • Ability to flourish and be effective in fast-paced settings
  • Flexibility to work as a team member and as a single contributor
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Suite
In addition, specific skills/experience required are as follows:
  • Servant Leader – You are hyper focused on engaging employees, fostering their development, and building a positive culture.
  • Solutions Focused – You see opportunities in every business problem and can develop, articulate, and implement solutions.
  • Collaboration – You are a relationship builder across all levels of the organization and across all business units.
  • Instills Trust - You do what you say, and you follow through on commitments, you act with integrity, you are consistent and are perceived as credible.
  • Impact & Influence Thinking – You gain support for ideas, proposals, and solutions, and get others to act, with or without formal authority, to advance initiatives/objectives.
About Sikich
Sikichis a global company specializing in Accounting, Advisory, and Technical professional services. With employees across the globe, Sikich ranks as one of the largest professional services companies in the United States. Our comprehensive skillsets, obtained over decades of experience as entrepreneurs, business owners and industry innovators, allow us to provide insights and transformative strategies to help strengthen every dimension of our clients’ businesses.
Sikich Total Rewards
Our team members enjoy expansive benefits ranging from competitive compensation and insurance options to wellness programs and a flexible time off policy, to name only a few. Sikich also takes pride in prioritizing team members’ health, total wellbeing and time spent with family, friends and in the pursuit of personal goals, hobbies, and endeavors.
In compliance with this state’s pay transparency laws, the midpoint of the salary range for this role is $116,104.63. This is not a guarantee of compensation or salary, as final offer amount may vary based on factors including but not limited to experience and geographic location.
Some examples of our many benefits:
  • Sikich maintains a Flexible Time Off (FTO) Policy. We encourage every full-time employee, as your role permits, to utilize paid time off (personal time, mental/physical health care, vacation, sick leave, etc.). Waiting for time off to accrue is common at other companies. At Sikich, you do not have to wait for this benefit to kick in. FTO is activated on your first day with our organization.
  • Sikich will also recognize paid holidays during the year and strives to permit employees to have time off the last week of the calendar year when client and project work permits. 
  • Sikich offers a comprehensive wellness program to engage, challenge and empower team members to take responsibility for their wellbeing. Activities can be tracked through our wellness provider to obtain gift cards and other rewards.
We also offer:
  • Flexible work arrangements
  • Health, dental, vision, life, and accident/death/disability insurance options
  • HSA employer contribution
  • Nine (9) paid holidays annually.
  • A robust paid Parental Bonding Leave program covering birth, adoption, and foster      children.
  • 401(k) with employer contributions
  • CPA bonus with four (4) paid exam days & four (4) paid study days.
  • Tuition reimbursement
  • Generous employee referral bonus program
  • Client referral bonus program
  • Pet insurance
  • FORCE – Sikich community volunteer program enabling each team member to use up to four hours of paid time annually to volunteer and make a difference in their local communities.
Want to learn more? Visit our Careers website or .
Sikich is an Equal Opportunity Employer M/F/D/V
Official correspondence will come from a Sikich.com email address and applicants/candidates are invited to contact us directly to verify communications
Sikich currently practices in an alternative practice structure in accordance with the AICPA Professional Code of Conduct and applicable law, regulations, and professional standards. Sikich CPA LLC is a licensed CPA firm that provides audit and attest services to its clients. Sikich LLC has a contractual arrangement with Sikich CPA LLC under which Sikich LLC provides Sikich CPA LLC with professional and support personnel and other services to support Sikich CPA LLC’s performance of its professional services, and Sikich CPA LLC shares certain client information with Sikich LLC with respect to the provision of such services.

jobs.title: VP, Private Credit Counsel
jobs.description:
Job Classification:

Corporate - Legal and Compliance

At Prudential, we believe talent is key to achieving our vision. When you join Prudential, you’ll unlock a motivating and impactful career – all while growing your skills and advancing your profession at one of the world’s leading financial services institutions!

PGIM Private Credit (“PPC”) is the private credit investment business of PGIM, Inc., the global investment management business of Prudential Financial, Inc. (NYSE: PRU) (“Prudential”) which ranks as one of the top asset managers in the world with approximately $1.47 trillion in assets under management as of December 31, 2025.  With eight US regional offices and seven non-US offices (in Europe, Australia and Mexico), PPC manages and sources new investments for PPC's $110 billion+ portfolio of privately placed debt and structured equity investments.  PPC offers to its global client base a broad range of private credit investment strategies that span the risk/return spectrum, from investment grade to below investment grade, and asset classes, from corporate financing to direct lending to mezzanine (corporate and energy-focused) to real assets (power, oil and gas, infrastructure and leasing). 

PPC’s Law department currently has 15 attorneys and is part of Prudential’s Law, Compliance, Business Ethics and External Affairs department.

PPC Law has an exciting opportunity to work in a dynamic global investment management business.  We are seeking an experienced corporate finance lawyer to primarily support PPC business units focusing on investment transactions.

The current EWA for this position is Hybrid and requires on-site presence on a reoccurring weekly basis as determined by the business.  The successful candidate will preferably work from a PPC office located in Atlanta, GA, Newark, NJ, Dallas, TX, Chicago, IL, Minneapolis, MN, Los Angeles, CA, or San Francisco, CA. We will provide additional details relative to the specific number of days you are expected to be on-site.   

What you can expect:

  • Structuring, negotiating and supervising the documentation of corporate finance transactions involving senior debt, mezzanine debt and/or equity
  • Serving as a strategic partner for PPC businesses in support of building and managing a varied investment portfolio
  • Building professional and working relationships with the leadership team and key stakeholders
  • Managing outside counsel
  • Tracking and monitoring regulatory trends that will have a meaningful impact on the business and strategizing with PPC management on appropriate actions 

What you'll need:

  • 6+ years of experience in corporate finance transactions, preferably with a major law firm or major financial institution
  • Prior experience representing (as inside or outside counsel) insurance companies, banks, private credit funds or other institutional investors in corporate finance and/or leveraged finance transactions
  • Familiarity or prior experience with the federal securities law 
  • Willingness and ability to work effectively with demanding PPC clients, borrowers, outside counsel and other institutional investors, as well as colleagues across the Prudential enterprise, is critical
  • Demonstrated strong leadership skills and the ability to work well in an environment where coordination and teamwork with legal and business associates is a priority
  • Admitted to practice law in at least one US jurisdiction and able to obtain an in-house counsel license, if required by state of office location

It’d be a plus if you had:

  • Experience with workouts, direct lending, project finance, credit tenant lease financing and/or infrastructure financing
  • Cross border/international experience
  • Foreign language skills

What we offer you:

Prudential is required by state specific laws to include the salary range for this role when hiring a resident in applicable locations. The salary range for this role is from $208,400.00 to $312,600.00. Specific pricing for the role may vary within the above range based on many factors including geographic location, candidate experience, and skills.

  • Market competitive base salaries, with a yearly bonus potential at every level. 

  • Medical, dental, vision, life insurance, disability insurance, Paid Time Off (PTO), and leave of absences, such as parental and military leave. 

  • 401(k) plan with company match (up to 4%). 

  • Company-funded pension plan. 

  • Wellness Programsincluding up to $1,600 a year for reimbursement of items purchased to support personal wellbeing needs. 

  • Work/Life Resources to help support topics such as parenting, housing, senior care, finances, pets, legal matters, education, emotional and mental health, and career development.  

  • Education Benefit to help finance traditional college enrollment toward obtaining an approved degree and many accredited certificate programs.  

  • Employee Stock Purchase Plan: Shares can be purchased at 85% of the lower of two prices (Beginning or End of the purchase period), after one year of service. 

Eligibility to participate in a discretionary annual incentive program is subject to the rules governing the program, whereby an award, if any, depends on various factors including, without limitation, individual and organizational performance. To find out more about our Total Rewards package, visit Work Life Balance | Prudential Careers. Some of the above benefits may not apply to part-time employees scheduled to work less than 20 hours per week. 

Prudential Financial, Inc. of the United States is not affiliated with Prudential plc. which is headquartered in the United Kingdom.

Prudential is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, genetics, disability, marital status, age, veteran status, domestic partner status, medical condition or any other characteristic protected by law.

If you need an accommodation to complete the application process, please email accommodations.hw@prudential.com.

If you are experiencing a technical issue with your application or an assessment, please email careers.technicalsupport@prudential.comto request assistance.

jobs.title: PGIM | Global Wealth | Portfolio Specialist (EMEA/APAC)
jobs.description:
Europe/Asia Portfolio Specialist

Global Wealth, London

A GLOBAL LEADING ASSET MANAGER WITH A DIVERSE & INCLUSIVE CULTURE

As the Global Asset Management business of Prudential, we’re always looking for ways to improve financial services. We’re passionate about making a meaningful impact - touching the lives of millions and solving financial challenges in an ever-changing world.

We also believe talent is key to achieving our vision and are intentional about building a culture on respect and collaboration. When you join PGIM, you’ll unlock a motivating and impactful career – all while growing your skills and advancing your profession at one of the world’s leading global asset managers! 

If you’re not afraid to think differently and challenge the status quo, come and be a part of a dedicated team that’s investing in your future by shaping tomorrow today.

At PGIM, You Can!

What you will do

You will be supporting PGIM’s Global Wealth Group in driving sales of PGIM’s Fixed Income UCITS, Sub-advisory and Alternatives platforms. The Portfolio Specialist will act as a central point of reference for PGIM Credit investment matters for PGIM’s sales force, clients, and prospects. The role involves developing a deep understanding of the firm’s investment strategies and delivering a high-quality service to clients and prospects, particularly as it relates to PGIM’s Fixed Income offering.

What you can expect

  • Partner and collaborate with the distribution teams to service wholesale and institutional clients across the region, providing technical fixed income expertise. Deliver timely investment strategy updates and ensure each prospect has a comprehensive understanding of PGIM’s capabilities and solutions.

  • Represent PGIM’s strategies at key conferences in support of the wholesale business.

  • Traveling as needed with the distribution teams to convey to prospects market perspectives, strategy updates and thought leadership.

  • Interact regularly with senior investment professionals and attend internal investment meetings to ensure deep and current understanding of PGIM’s investment strategies positioning and outlook.

  • Partnering with internal groups to ensure business development and client needs are met.

  • Ensure clients and internal partners are fully informed, trained, and supported on PGIM’s managed products and capabilities.

  • Collaborate with sales, marketing, and product teams to create and refine compelling product materials, presentations, and thought leadership content.

  • Provide guidance and training to members of Institutional Client Group and Global Wealth on Fixed Income strategies.

What you will bring

  • Significant buy-side asset management experience working in similar roles.

  • Extensive experience in fixed income and a demonstrable ability to grasp and communicate fixed income concepts in a succinct and digestible manner.

  • Strong presentation skills which can help drive clients and prospects to action and strengthen and build relationships with PGIM.

  • An understanding of investment processes and the ability to effectively convey key differentiators to clients, prospects and sales teams via both verbal and written communications.

  • An ability to effectively grasp important, value-added aspects of each strategy that will enable the candidate to successfully represent PGIM capabilities to a sophisticated client/ prospect base.

  • Genuine team player with a collaborative style; able to build partnerships and relationships based on trust and credibility. Likewise, confidence and presence to work with senior executives.

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities, with a passion for continuous learning and staying ahead of industry trends.

  • Highly responsive, flexible and oriented toward meeting and exceeding the needs of clients and prospects.

  • A strong grasp of data analysis with the ability to interpret and convey results in a digestible manner.

What will set you apart?

  • Master’s degree in finance or equivalent

  • CFA certification

  • European languages (Italian and/or German) strongly desirable.

*PGIM welcomes all applicants, even if you don't meet every requirement. If your skills align with the role, we encourage you to apply.

What we offer you

  • Private Medical Insurance: PGIM offers private medical insurance and pays the full premium on behalf of all employees.
  • Annual Leave: 25 to 28 days, based on years of service, at full pay.
  • Retirement Savings Plan: Employees receive a company contribution of 10% basic annual salary and a maximum employer-matching contribution of 5% of basic annual salary.
  • Life Assurance & Income Protection: Life Assurance for 8 times your basic annual salary, up to a max benefit of £1.8million. 60% of your basic annual salary for a limited payment terms of 5 years, at the end of which a capital sum of 2x your basic annual salary is payable.

To find out more about our Total Reward package, visit https://youtu.be/acuhcv46Nz0

About Global Wealth

Global Wealth provides access to active investment strategies across the global markets in the pursuit of consistent outperformance for investors. We’re part of PGIM, a leading global investment manager.

Global Wealth offers actively managed investment solutions including US mutual funds; closed-end funds; ETFs; UCITS funds; PGIM Custom Harvest; and Asia Local.

Our broad range of investment solutions allows us to deliver a diversified suite of actively managed solutions across a broad spectrum of asset classes and investment styles to meet investors’ long-term needs. We provide the focus, expertise, innovation, and attention to risk demanded by the most sophisticated investors through products managed by our affiliated asset managers: affiliated asset managers: PGIM Public and Private Fixed Income, PGIM Real Estate, Jennison Associates, and PGIM Quantitative Solutions

Learn more about PGIM Global Wealth

About PGIM – Global Asset Management

PGIM is the global asset management business of Prudential Financial, Inc. (NYSE: PRU), a leading global investment manager with nearly US$1.5 trillion in assets under management as of September 30th, 2025.  With offices in 19 countries, PGIM’s businesses offer a range of investment solutions for retail and institutional investors around the world across a broad range of asset classes, including public fixed income, private fixed income, fundamental equity, quantitative equity, real estate and alternatives.

With a history dating back 150 years, and experience through more than 30 market cycles, PGIM takes a long-term view, not only in our investment philosophy, but also in how we develop our talent. We want to see our employees excel from their first day with the firm and throughout their tenure with PGIM. We will inspire you, support you, and help you reach your greatest personal and professional aspirations. If PGIM sounds like the place for you, join us. For more information about PGIM, visit PGIM.com.

Prudential Financial, Inc. of the United States is not affiliated with Prudential plc. which is headquartered in the United Kingdom.

Our Commitment to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion

PGIM has a long-held commitment to advancing diversity, equity and inclusion, rooted in the belief that this is a prerequisite for PGIM's success and sustainability. We work to attract, retain, develop and promote talent at all levels and aim to have a workforce that reflects the communities where we work, live and invest. We are intentional about creating a workplace where everyone has a voice and is welcomed, valued, respected and understood. From the moment people join PGIM, we pledge to help them unlock their full potential. Together, we are shaping a future where talent knows no boundaries.

jobs.title: PGIM | Global Wealth | Alternative Investment Specialist
jobs.description:
Alternative Investment Specialist

London, Global Wealth

A GLOBAL LEADING ASSET MANAGER WITH A DIVERSE & INCLUSIVE CULTURE

As the Global Asset Management business of Prudential, we’re always looking for ways to improve financial services. We’re passionate about making a meaningful impact - touching the lives of millions and solving financial challenges in an ever-changing world.

We also believe talent is key to achieving our vision and are intentional about building a culture on respect and collaboration. When you join PGIM, you’ll unlock a motivating and impactful career – all while growing your skills and advancing your profession at one of the world’s leading global asset managers! 

If you’re not afraid to think differently and challenge the status quo, come and be a part of a dedicated team that’s investing in your future by shaping tomorrow today.

At PGIM, You Can!

What you will do

PGIM’s Global Wealth Team seeks to hire an experienced, field-facing International Alternative Investments Sales Specialist (“AI Sales Specialist”) to support the ongoing growth of its $350 billion alternative investment platform, which spans a wide range of assets classes and strategies, across private and public markets. The AI Sales Specialist will collaborate closely with key stakeholders in investment, product management, marketing and sales teams to distribute alternative investment solutions to diverse investors across EMEA and APAC wealth channels. The position will be based in London, England and report to Dominick Carlino, Global Head of Alternative Investments, PGIM Global Wealth.

What you can expect

  • Distribution Support: Provide subject matter expertise and partner with International Sales teams to execute educational and sales initiatives for alternative strategies across wealth management firms, private banks, retail banks and family offices. Adapt US-developed strategies for regulatory and market nuances across EMEA/APAC.
  • Sales Enablement: Partner with International Sales teams to lead roadshows and campaigns, supporting the creation of multilingual marketing collateral and educational content.
  • Client Engagement: Represent PGIM’s alternatives platform in client meetings, conferences, and thematic forums. Deliver tailored presentations and thought leadership on global alternatives trends.
  • Client Service: Ensure timely and high-quality post-sales support, researching and resolving advisor and client matters  with consistency and excellence. 
  • Product Strategy: Collaborate with global product teams to design region-specific solutions using alternative investment structures such as private placements, feeder funds, limited partnerships, interval funds, and offshore vehicles. Monitor competitive landscape and investor preferences to inform product positioning.
  • Governance & Compliance: Ensure adherence to local regulatory frameworks (FCA, ESMA) and cross-border distribution rules.

What you will bring

  • 10+ years in alternatives distribution or product strategy within global asset management and private wealth
  • Deep expertise in private markets and hedge fund strategies; strong knowledge of alternative structures (private placements, feeder funds, evergreen funds, offshore partnerships).
  • Proven ability to communicate across cultures and navigate diverse regulatory environments.
  • CAIA/CFA preferred; proficiency in European and/or Asian languages highly desirable.

*PGIM welcomes all applicants, even if you don't meet every requirement. If your skills align with the role, we encourage you to apply.

What we offer you

  • Private Medical Insurance: PGIM offers private medical insurance and pays the full premium on behalf of all employees.
  • Annual Leave of 25 to 28 days, based on years of service, at full pay.
  • Retirement Savings Plan: Employees receive a company contribution of 10% basic annual salary and a maximum employer-matching contribution of 5% of basic annual salary.
  • Life Assurance & Income Protection: Life Assurance for 8 times your basic annual salary, up to a max benefit of £1.8million. 60% of your basic annual salary for a limited payment terms of 5 years, at the end of which a capital sum of 2x your basic annual salary is payable.

To find out more about our Total Reward package, visit https://youtu.be/acuhcv46Nz0

Global Wealth provides access to active investment strategies across the global markets in the pursuit of consistent outperformance for investors. We’re part of PGIM, a leading global investment manager.

Global Wealth offers actively managed investment solutions including US mutual funds; closed-end funds; ETFs; UCITS funds; PGIM Custom Harvest; and Asia Local.

Our broad range of investment solutions allows us to deliver a diversified suite of actively managed solutions across a broad spectrum of asset classes and investment styles to meet investors’ long-term needs. We provide the focus, expertise, innovation, and attention to risk demanded by the most sophisticated investors through products managed by our affiliated asset managers: affiliated asset managers: PGIM Public and Private Fixed Income, PGIM Real Estate, Jennison Associates, and PGIM Quantitative Solutions

jobs.title: Associate Director, Asset Management
jobs.description:
Purpose and Job Summary:


The Associate Director will be a key member of the ORIX Growth Capital team in New York, focusing on underwriting and portfolio management.  Key elements of the role include company and investment evaluation, industry and economic research, analysis of micro- and macroeconomic trends impacting company performance, term sheet review, company-specific and industry-wide due diligence, financial modeling, legal documentation and close, focusing on underwriting and portfolio management.  The successful candidate will work closely with Senior Management of ORIX Growth Capital, equity investors and management teams to successfully grow the ORIX Growth Capital brand. ORIX Growth Capital is proud of its talented team, strong industry relationships and commitment to long term success. Candidates must have a strong interest in technology companies and a desire to work closely with venture capital and private equity communities.

Essential Duties and Responsibilities:

  • Perform industry and relevant economic research in order to analyze relevant trends and key risks on investment opportunities
  • Communicate key industry and economic trends impacting current and future company performance both verbally and via written investment memorandums and research reports
  • Evaluate potential investment opportunities and create financial models using company-specific/industry-wide factors and overall short- and long-term economic trends, and discuss relevant impacts to the investment
  • Serve a primary lead in due diligence efforts, including company-specific, industry-wide and macroeconomic risks and opportunities, as well as collecting and analyzing relevant data and trends
  • Prepare term sheets, credit applications, and other documentation required for approval of deal by investment committee and executive management
  • Coordinate legal review and documentation for deal closing
  • Build and nurture ongoing relationships with new and existing clients and investors
  • Work with ORIX Growth Capital portfolio companies, including monitoring of performance, attending board meetings, and following up on investment
  • Some domestic travel required, dependent on investment activities
  • Other duties and projects, as assigned

Contacts

This position has frequent contact with all levels of employees and management. In addition, this role may interact with outside business partners, vendors, consultants, and other office visitors.

Education, Skills and Experience:

  • Bachelor’s degree from accredited four-year university in finance, economics, or related field required; Master's degree preferred
  • 5+ years of experience at a top-tier investment bank, private equity or debt fund, venture capital firm or growth technology company
  • Deep understanding of software & technology industries, and venture & growth ecosystems
  • Highly developed analytical skills with ability to define issues, collect and analyze pertinent data, identify alternative solutions, project consequences of proposed actions, draw valid conclusions, propose sound recommendations, and implement approved solutions in support of specified goals. Attention to detail is critical
  • Excellent proficiency in accounting and financial modeling
  • Exceptional organizational, communication, and presentation skills, including the ability to prepare comprehensive and insightful investment memoranda
  • Solid interpersonal skills with success as an approachable collaborator at all levels of an organization
  • Demonstrated ability to plan, organize, and prioritize assignments, and to meet critical and established deadlines; Ability to manage multiple changing assignments simultaneously without constant supervision
  • A strong team orientation and the ability to thrive in a fun, performance-driven environment
  • Ability to travel, as needed, for investment activities, sometimes with short notice

Annual base salary gross: $160,000 - $200,000 (CA only).  The base salary range represents the estimated low and high end of ORIX’s salary for this position based on geography. Actual base salaries will vary and will be based on various factors, such as candidate’s qualifications, skills, competencies and proficiency for the role. The base pay is one component of ORIX’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards and benefits include cash bonuses, long term incentives, health insurance, accident and life insurance, and retirement benefits.  Please visit our Benefits page for additional information.

Life at ORIX

We are committed to the health and well-being of our employees and offer a quality, competitive benefits package that provides valuable healthcare and financial protection for our workforce, while emphasizing a healthy work/life balance.

You Time

We want you to have the time needed to recharge and manage life with our competitive vacation policy, floating personal holidays, extensive leave options, our annual observed holidays, and more.

Family Care

Every family has a different journey and ORIX USA Group is proud to offer extensive benefit programs such as: infertility coverage, adoption support, child and elder care, fully paid maternity, and generous parental leave benefits to help you and your families along the way.

Flexible Work Arrangements

ORIX associates enjoy the advantages of our hybrid working model with a 3 day in-office minimum requirement.

ORIX USA is an equal opportunity employer, providing equal employment opportunities (EEO), and is committed to diversifying its workforce. All employees and applicants for employment will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, genetics, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy, protected veteran status or other status protected by law. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment.

jobs.title: Senior Director, Asset Management
jobs.description:
Purpose and Job Summary:

ORIX Growth Capital is seeking a Senior Director or Director for our San Francisco office to help continue successfully growing the ORIX Growth Capital platform, particularly in the Bay Area. This investment professional will have responsibility for new investments and investment performance including establishing and deepening relationships with venture capital and private equity funds, technology firms, and intermediaries, identifying and winning quality financings, leading diligence and investment approval, and assisting with ongoing portfolio success. ORIX Growth Capital is proud of its talented team, strong industry relationships, and commitment to long-term success.

ORIX Growth Capital is one of the leading growth lending platforms in the market. We provide flexible debt and equity capital to valuable, high-growth businesses, primarily in the technology and healthcare sectors in the U.S. and Canada.

Essential Duties and Responsibilities:

  • Originate new investment opportunities that have a strong risk/return and are a fit for our investment strategy. Build and nurture a wide and deep network of origination relationships
  • Develop a complete understanding and the risk/return of an emerging growth company by evaluating the entity’s market opportunity, competition, product, sales and growth, operations, financial statements and projections, management strength, equity support, capital structure, and collateral
  • Extensive business development, transaction identification, analysis, and underwriting
  • Evaluation and recommendation must be synthesized in a clear and concise investment memo for investment decisions with investment committee
  • Monitor the financial performance and compliance of portfolio accounts, remain current on industry trends and competitive landscape, monitor the venture markets for relevant news, review legal documents, and collaborate with senior management in the growth and success of ORIX Growth Capital
  • Other projects and duties as needed
  • Some travel may be required.


Education, Skills and Experience:

Required:

  • Bachelor’s degree from an accredited four-year university in finance, economics, or related field required; Master's degree preferred
  • 10+ years of venture and growth company, equity and debt investing, and/or investment banking expertise
  • Prior direct investing experience required; Lending/Credit or venture capital experience a plus
  • Wide range network in the venture and growth equity and growth company ecosystem
  • Knowledge of the venture and growth company sector with technology and/or healthcare sector expertise
  • Strong accounting/financial modeling and analytical skills supported by a high proficiency in Microsoft Excel, Word, and PowerPoint
  • Ability to comfortably work with multiple concurrent transactions
  • Sound business judgment and decision-making abilities
  • Excellent organizational, communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills
  • Ability to plan, organize, and prioritize assignments, and to meet critical and established deadlines; Ability to manage multiple assignments simultaneously
  • Ability to learn quickly and demonstrated ability to work independently and as part of a high-performance team
  • Flexibility and ability to work under pressure with tight deadlines and constant interruptions; Ability to adjust priorities in a rapidly changing environment


Annual base salary gross: $220,000 - $300,000 (CA only). The base salary range represents the estimated low and high end of ORIX’s salary for this position based on geography. Actual base salaries will vary and will be based on various factors, such as candidate’s qualifications, skills, competencies and proficiency for the role. The base pay is one component of ORIX’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards and benefits include cash bonuses, long term incentives, health insurance, accident and life insurance, and retirement benefits.  Please visit our Benefits page for additional information.

jobs.title: Senior Director Debt Capital Markets
jobs.description:
Purpose and Job Summary

Lument provides comprehensive capital solutions for multifamily, affordable, seniors and health care real estate.  This includes a full suite of financing and strategic solutions such as investment banking, mortgage banking, private equity, a variety of direct balance sheet lending options, investment sales, and mergers and acquisitions.  The Debt Capital Markets (DCM) group is seeking a Senior Director responsible for developing strategic lending partners (SLP’s) focused heavily on life company lending through correspondent relationships, serving as the main point of contact for maintaining the relationships. This position will contribute to the growth of the business by leveraging unique life company lending products that will create valuable options to meet the lending needs of Lument borrowers focusing on all CRE asset classes. The Senior Director will be an integral part of the team as a player coach for the DCM Central Desk staff, responsible for a variety of areas related to SLP and life company products, marketing products internally to the origination platform, and serving as the product knowledge expert for both front end and back-end execution.

Essential Duties & Responsibilities

  • Serve as life company relationship and product manager
  • Leverage life company relationships and contacts to develop a stable of partners and correspondent relationships
  • Represent the firm to life company partners at industry events and through in person meetings
  • Perform review of life company loan submission packages for acceptability
  • Reviews lender submission packages from analysts/associates for deal placement to life companies
  • Reviews quotes and application packages from analyst/associates
  • Provides workflow oversight for life company deals in process and in servicing as needed
  • Manages Central Desk and directs associates/analysts in all facets of analysis and preparing deals for life company and other execution

Provides credible recommendations to supervisors and senior management based on market research and analysis

  • Monitors and assists originator and support staff throughout the due diligence process and closing process as needed
  • Demonstrate excellent client relations skills
  • Work professional and harmoniously with team and coworkers
  • Other projects and duties as assigned

Travel Requirements

15% - In person meetings with strategic lending partners and conferences as appropriate

Contacts

This position has frequent contact with all levels of employees and management. In addition, this role interacts with outside business partners, vendors, consultants, and other office visitors

Education, Skills & Experience Required

  • Bachelor’s degree from an accredited college or university required.
  • Minimum of 10 years of experience in the life company commercial mortgage lending industry.
  • Management or supervisory experience with a small team.
  • Strong proficiency with MS-Excel, word, Argus, and other computer programs needed.

Preferred

  • Product knowledge of CRE lending products across all asset classes.
  • Experienced with Bank, CMBS, Debt Fund, and Agency lending products.

jobs.title: Director, Infrastructure & Public Finance - Originations
jobs.description:
Job Summary:
ORIX Corporation USA’s Infrastructure & Public Finance group (IPF) is seeking an experienced originator to source high-yield private credit investments. The successful candidate will focus on securing land development opportunities for investment, primarily in Texas and the US Sunbelt states.

Key Responsibilities

  • Origination: Lead the end-to-end sourcing and closing of high-yield debt opportunities.

  • Execution: Leverage a deep understanding of risk underwriting and complex deal structuring to drive portfolio growth.

  • Networking: Utilize existing relationships with real estate developers to expand ORIX’s footprint in the residential and commercial sectors.

Candidate Qualifications

  • Proven Track Record: Demonstrated success in originating and closing high-yield private credit or real estate transactions.

  • Strategic Network: An established contact list of real estate developers.

  • Professional Background: Experience in Investment Banking, Private Credit, or Residential/Commercial Real Estate Development lending.

  • Risk Analysis:  Assist in the preparation and presentation of credit presentations for approval committees. Work as part of a team to develop independent and fact-based investment recommendations.

Education:

  • Bachelor’s degree from accredited four-year university in finance, economics, or related field required

  • Minimum 10 years of financial advisory, real estate development or investment banking experience

  • Master’s degree from accredited university preferred, CPA or CFA beneficial but not required.

About the IPF Team

The Infrastructure & Public Finance group is an innovative, flexible investor based in Dallas. The 14-person team provides senior and subordinate debt and preferred equity capital across a diverse range of public and private financings in a highly collaborative environment.

jobs.title: Pricing & Valuation Associate
jobs.description:
At Barings, we are as invested in our associates as we are in our clients. We recognize those who work diligently for us and reward them for personal and professional integrity, communication skills, distinct competencies and expertise in specific strategies, ability to collaborate as a team member and true dedication to the interests of our clients.

We thank you for your interest in joining the Barings team, and invite you to explore our current employment opportunities.

Title:  Associate Pricing & Valuation

Department: Pricing & Valuation

Location: London, UK

Barings is a leading global financial services firm dedicated to meeting the evolving investment and capital needs of our clients and customers. Through active asset management and direct origination, we provide innovative solutions and access to differentiated opportunities across public and private capital markets. A subsidiary of MassMutual, Barings maintains a strong global presence with business and investment professionals located across North America, Europe and Asia Pacific.

Job Summary

Barings is seeking a motivated individual with strong analytical skills and a strong attention to detail for the European Pricing/Valuation team. This individual will be responsible for key components of our diverse asset type valuations, including fixed income, equity, and private assets.

The role requires working directly across global departments within Barings, including traders, investment analysts, portfolio managers, fund accountants and other areas with operations. This position also includes review of Fair Value and Swing Pricing calculations prepared by team members.

Primary Responsibilities

  • Ensuring the Fair Valuation of Barings invested securities and the adherence to the Barings Pricing Policy
  • Preparing daily fair valuation instructions to the third party administrator
  • Pricing and monitoring of unlisted, illiquid, suspended, and distressed assets.
  • Calculating Swing Pricing factors and thresholds, and ensuring adherence to the Swing Pricing policies and procedures
  • Regular reporting to the Barings Pricing Committee, including attending and presenting at Committee meetings
  • Checking and verifying of team members’ inputs
  • Responding to global internal business partners as well as fund custodians and administrators.
  • Reviewing daily exception reports, validating output and overall review for accuracy. Team plays an integral role within the organization interacting with internal partners, portfolio managers, and trading desks.

Skills/Experience Required

  • Familiarity with a broad range of asset types
  • Fund Administration/Operations experience - Valuations related exposure a plus
  • Good understanding of global financial markets and current events
  • Intermediate knowledge of Microsoft Office, particularly Excel
  • Excellent communication skills and ability to work independently as well as on a team
  • Strong initiative, high attention to detail, and ability to respond quickly to issues are attributes a candidate must possess.

Desired Skills & Qualifications

  • Skills and experience using SQL, PowerBI, Aladdin, and VBA
  • IOC / IMC qualified or similar
  • Degree in Accounting, Finance or related field.
  • Swing Pricing and Fair Value experience

#LI-JS1

Requisite Skills

Additional Skills

Job title: Portfolio Analytics, Senior Associate

Department: Portfolio Finance

Location: Charlotte, NC or Boston, MA

The Opportunity

The Portfolio Analytics Senior Associate is a key contributor towards operational effectiveness and efficiency leveraging technology-based solutions to streamline efficiencies, enable insightful program-level reporting, and support the reduction of investment and operational risk. This role sits within Barings Portfolio Finance’s Portfolio Analytics team, a team responsible for Data & Analytics, Operational Infrastructure, Management Reporting, Track Record, and Credit Modeling. The role operates at the intersection of finance and technology and serves as a key liaison with various internal partners.

The Team

The team is small, close-knit, and highly collaborative. Team members are motivated by a shared goal to originate attractive investment opportunities for third-party clients while addressing the unique financing needs of our customers. The team is primarily focused on originating investment-grade, senior secured loans to asset managers and the investment vehicles that they manage across a range of asset classes, including private equity, private credit, and real estate debt. The loans, directly originated by the Portfolio Finance team, are highly negotiated, and often involve complex structuring across various global jurisdictions. The team has originated over $38 billion in private investments since 2017 and recently transitioned to Barings from MassMutual.

Key Responsibilities

  • Perform routine data cleansing/pre-processing and automate data transformation processes.
  • Support with maintenance of BPF’s proprietary portfolio monitoring database and assessing data quality & integrity on a continuous basis.
  • Identify, analyze, and interpret financial data and uncover trends within large/complex data sets.
  • Identify various process improvement solutions and support with development of predictive analytic capabilities.
  • Critically evaluate information provided from multiple sources – both internal and external – and reconcile discrepancies to ensure sound data quality.
  • Produce (and automate where possible) regularly scheduled and ad-hoc management reports to enable decision making.
  • Participate in technology/automation initiatives to drive streamlined integration with borrowers, internal partners, and third-party vendors.
  • Support internal controls activities.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree (business and/or data science/analytics related) with 2 - 4 years of experience in data management within a financial context; solid financial acumen.
  • Minimum of 2 years’ experience in data analysis or reporting.

  • Experience with data visualization tools & best practices (hands on experience with PowerBi or Tableau preferred).

  • Strong knowledge of and experience with analytic tools and programming languages (Python/R, and SQL with VBA a plus).

  • Excellent command and proficiency of MS excel.

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, attention to detail, well-organized, self-starter, and able to multitask and deliver work products on short timeframes.
  • Willingness to learn and absorb complex lending structures and legal documents and translate economic and risk factors to financial models and tools used to manage the overall portfolio.
  • Experience with synthesizing and visualizing data for presentations.
  • Ability and openness to understanding operational, accounting, and legal aspects of investments.
  • Demonstrated interest in and experience with operational enablement through technology.
  • Excellent documentation and communication skills.
  • Control focused and attentive to detail.

Ideal Qualifications

  • Prior experience in investment reporting and analysis within private credit highly preferred.
  • Experience working with at least one alternative asset class and a good understanding of the risks and operations of others across the alternative investment landscape.

#LI-CM2

Base Salary Range: $90,000-105,000 and additional incentive compensation.

Requisite Skills

Data Analytics, Reporting and Analysis

Additional Skills

Barings is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer; Minority/Female/Age/Sexual Orientation/Gender Identity/Individual with Disability/Protected Veteran. We welcome all persons to apply.

Barings offers a comprehensive benefits package including:

CORE BENEFITS & WELLNESS

  • Medical (including Virtual Care), Prescription, Dental, and Vision Coverage
  • Fitness Center Reimbursement Program (Including Online Memberships)
  • Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
  • Fertility Benefits

FINANCIAL WELL-BEING

  • Highly competitive 401(k) Plan with Company Match
  • Health Savings Account (HSA) with Company Contributions
  • Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA) - Health Care & Dependent Care
  • Retirement Health Reimbursement Account

LIFE INSURANCE

  • Basic and Supplemental Life Insurance
  • Spouse and Child Life Insurance

TIME OFF, DISABILITY AND LEAVE OF ABSENCE

  • Paid Vacation, Sick Days and Annual Holidays
  • Paid Leave of Absences (Maternity Leave, Parental Leave, Caregiver Leave, Bereavement Time)
  • Short and Long Term Disability Plans
  • Paid Volunteer Time

 OTHER BENEFITS

  • Education Assistance Program
  • Charitable Matching Gifts Program
  • Commuter Reimbursement Program
  • Adoption and Surrogacy Reimbursement Program

jobs.description:

Job Title: Commercial ABS, Credit Research Analyst

Corporate Title: Director

Department: Asset Backed Finance

Location: New York, Charlotte, or Boston

Overview

Barings is seeking a highly motivated and experienced professional to join its ABF (Asset-Backed Finance) team as a Director, Credit Research Analyst specializing in Commercial ABS transactions. The individual will be responsible for leading credit research, analysis, and underwriting of private and public market commercial ABS transactions with a dedicated focus on Transportation (Aviation, Rail), Digital Infrastructure (Data Centers, Fiber), and Esoteric ABS (Franchise, Whole Business) sectors.

This position requires deep expertise in evaluating complex asset-backed structures, conducting comprehensive credit analysis of underlying collateral, and making informed investment recommendations across the capital structure. The individual will work closely with portfolio managers, investment professionals, risk partners, and internal stakeholders to identify attractive investment opportunities, support transaction structuring and execution, and monitor portfolio performance. This role demands strong analytical capabilities, excellent communication skills, and the ability to develop credit views independently while collaborating effectively across a global team.

Key Responsibilities

Credit Research & Analysis

  • Lead comprehensive credit analysis and due diligence of commercial ABS transactions across Transportation, Digital Infrastructure, and Esoteric ABS  sectors.
  • Evaluate originator and servicer capabilities, business models, operational track records, and credit risk management frameworks.
  • Analyze collateral pools, performance data, vintage trends, and stratifications to assess credit quality and projected performance.
  • Assess legal and structural elements of ABS transactions, including waterfall mechanics, credit enhancement features, triggers, and covenants.
  • Develop independent credit views on asset fundamentals, including technology trends, regulatory developments, competitive dynamics, and business model risks.

Investment Evaluation & Execution

  • Screen, underwrite, and support approval of new investments in both public and private commercial ABS markets.
  • Prepare detailed investment memos, exhibits, and presentations for internal approval and investment committee processes.
  • Perform scenario, sensitivity, and stress analyses to evaluate structural resilience, downside protection, and expected returns across economic cycles.
  • Utilize cash flow models and analytical tools to form opinions on future asset and transaction performance.
  • Engage directly with issuers, arrangers, bankers, originators, and other transaction stakeholders throughout the investment process.

Portfolio Surveillance & Monitoring

  • Monitor ongoing performance of existing investments versus underwritten expectations and identify emerging risks or opportunities.
  • Analyze monthly/quarterly collateral and transaction performance reports, including delinquencies, defaults, losses, prepayments, and structural triggers.
  • Recommend actions based on asset performance trends, covenant compliance, and structural changes.
  • Maintain regular dialogue with issuers and servicers to stay informed on portfolio developments and operational updates.

Research, Thought Leadership & Collaboration

  • Author sector research, market commentary, and thematic reports on sector trends.
  • Contribute to analytical frameworks, underwriting standards, and investment guidelines for commercial ABS sectors.
  • Represent the firm at industry conferences, investor meetings, and client engagements.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional partners including portfolio management, risk, operations, legal, finance, and technology teams.
  • Mentor and support junior team members, fostering analytical rigor and professional development.

Qualifications

Education & Experience

  • Bachelor's degree in Finance, Economics, Engineering, Mathematics, Accounting, or a related quantitative discipline preferred.
  • 7–15 years of relevant experience in structured finance, asset-backed securities, commercial credit analysis, or specialty finance, with demonstrated expertise in Transportation, Digital Infrastructure, and/or Esoteric ABS sectors.
  • Direct experience analyzing and underwriting commercial ABS transactions in both public and private markets is highly preferred.
  • Buy-side, sell-side, rating agency, or lending experience in asset-backed finance or structured credit is valued.

Technical Skills & Knowledge

  • Strong Excel-based financial modeling and analytical skills with a disciplined, well-structured, and detail-oriented approach.
  • Deep understanding of commercial ABS structures, collateral performance analytics, and credit fundamentals.
  • Experience with cash flow modeling platforms such as Intex, or similar tools.
  • Proficiency with Bloomberg, data analysis tools, and transaction databases.
  • Familiarity with Transportation, Digital Infrastructure, and/or Esoteric ABS asset classes, including knowledge of industry fundamentals, regulatory frameworks, and performance drivers.
  • Programming or data analytics skills (e.g., Python, SQL, R, VBA) are beneficial but not required.

Professional Attributes

  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with demonstrated ability to clearly articulate complex credit views and analytical findings to senior stakeholders, investment committees, and external counterparties.
  • Strong attention to detail, sound judgment, and intellectual curiosity with the ability to independently develop credit opinions.
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities and projects simultaneously in a fast-paced, dynamic environment while maintaining high-quality analytical outputs.
  • Collaborative mindset with strong interpersonal skills and a team-oriented work ethic.
  • Results-oriented and self-motivated with a passion for credit analysis and structured finance.

Preferred Qualifications

  • CFA charter holder, CFA candidate, or other relevant professional designations (e.g., CAIA, FRM).
  • Advanced degree (MBA, MS Finance, or related field) is a plus.
  • Established relationships and credibility with market participants including issuers, arrangers, investors, and industry participants in Transportation, Digital Infrastructure, and Esoteric ABS markets.
  • Thoughtful perspectives on sector trends, technological developments, regulatory changes, and market dynamics impacting commercial ABS asset classes.

#LI-CM2

Asset-Backed Securities (ABS), Credit Analysis, Credit Research, Investment Evaluation, Portfolio Monitoring

Title: Director, Client Portfolio Management – Private Credit

Business Unit: Client Portfolio Management

Location: Singapore or Hong Kong

Barings is a leading global financial services firm dedicated to meeting the evolving investment and capital needs of our clients and customers. Through active asset management and direct origination, we provide innovative solutions and access to differentiated opportunities across public and private capital markets. A subsidiary of MassMutual, Barings maintains a strong global presence with business and investment professionals located across North America, Europe, and Asia Pacific.

Job Summary

We are looking for a Director, Client Portfolio Management to join in our Singapore office. Client Portfolio Managers work in close partnership and coordination with investment teams and sales as well as other stakeholders within the organization as subject matter experts, focusing on business development, client servicing and product development for certain investment strategies.

This position will preferably be located in Singapore, although candidates in Hong Kong will also be considered. The role will support all Barings private credit strategies (portfolio finance, infrastructure debt, asset-based finance, private placements, direct lending, capital solutions) with a primary focus on portfolio finance and infrastructure debt.

Primary Responsibilities

  • Work with product management, investment teams and sales teams to drive new and existing business development in private credit strategies (and portfolio finance and infrastructure debt in particular) in the APAC region.
  • Develop a close partnership with investment teams to deepen knowledge and expertise and be effective as a portfolio manager proxy in interactions with sales colleagues and clients. This will include:
    • Attending investment team meetings
    • Serving as an active participant in meetings with current and prospective clients
    • Responding in a timely manner to client and prospect requests
  • Communicate investment strategies internally and externally with the goal of growing assets under management
  • Help develop, maintain and prepare marketing and servicing materials for the team. This will include pitch books as well as scheduled and ad-hoc updates on private credit markets and specific aspects of an investment strategy (e.g. monthly commentaries, market reviews, market outlook, performance reviews, portfolio positioning etc).
  • Review and provide strategy specific responses to RFPs and DDQs
  • Identify, participate in and coordinate new product development and positioning initiatives
  • Create thought leadership materials in cooperation with investment teams and Barings Marketing staff

Qualifications

  • University degree
  • 7+ years of relevant work experience, ideally in an investment specialist/product strategist/client portfolio manager or similar role.
  • A passion for financial markets. Private credit markets experience a strong plus.
  • Excellent oral and written communication skills including an ability to assess and convey market dynamics, portfolio strategy and risk factors
  • An aptitude for public speaking in a variety of settings and audience sizes
  • An entrepreneurial drive and desire to be a team player
  •  Strong organizational, facilitation and collaboration skills
  •  Willingness to travel regularly within the APAC region.

Requisite Skills

Additional Skills

Barings is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer; Minority/Female/Age/Sexual Orientation/Gender Identity/Individual with Disability/Protected Veteran. We welcome all persons to apply.

Title: Loan Asset Management

Business Title: Associate/Senior Associate

Business Unit: Portfolio Finance

Location: London, UK (On-site)

Barings is a leading global financial services firm dedicated to meeting the evolving investment and capital needs of our clients and customers. Through active asset management and direct origination, we provide innovative solutions and access to differentiated opportunities across public and private capital markets. A subsidiary of MassMutual, Barings maintains a strong global presence with business and investment professionals located across North America, Europe, and Asia Pacific.

Job Summary:

Responsible for supporting the loan activities of complex portfolio level loan facilities through the loan lifecycle.

Team Overview:

Barings Portfolio Finance provides customized, complex, highly negotiated, proprietary secured loans to private asset managers and manages the entire loan lifecycle.  As a member of Portfolio Finance’s loan asset management team you will support the investment team, borrowers, and external stakeholders – from origination to offboarding.

Primary Responsibilities

  • Assist with loan activities at closing and onboarding of new loan facilities including review of credit agreements, loan & servicing agreements, amendments, account control agreements, and closing/settlement statements in relation to Loan Asset Management functions and best practices
  • On-going servicing and other administration functions on serviced loans including compliance and covenant monitoring
  • Utilize financial models to record collateral data, test compliance metrics, and calculate waterfalls
  • Support treasury & investment operations activities through cash and reporting reconciliations
  • Ensure deliverables are managed to a high standard and within timelines

Qualifications

  • Degree in Business, Finance, related field or equivalent
  • 3+ years of relevant investment operations/support experience preferably with investing or servicing private equity, private real estate, or private credit and lending documents
  • Ability to develop credibility and build relationships with all internal and external stakeholders and partners
  • Excellent attention to detail  
  • Demonstrate exceptional project/time management, coordination, and organizational skills
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
  • Moderate to Advanced use of Excel and proficient use of Microsoft Office Suite

Requisite Skills

Legal Documents, Relationship Building, Sharp Attention to Detail (Inactive), Team Working (Inactive), Time Management

Additional Skills

Deal Closing, Microsoft Excel, Project Management

Job Title: Real Estate Equity, Senior Associate

Corporate Title: Senior Associate

Department: Real Estate Equity

Location: El Segundo, CA

We are seeking a highly motivated Senior Associate to join our Real Estate Equity team, supporting acquisitions and asset management activities across the Eastern US. This individual will be deeply involved in analyzing new investment opportunities, maintaining and enhancing valuation models, preparing investment materials, and supporting execution from underwriting through closing.

This is an opportunity to join a well‑capitalized, high‑performing platform with meaningful exposure to senior leadership, investment committees, and cross‑functional real estate teams. The role offers hands‑on experience across acquisitions, valuation, leasing analysis, and portfolio performance.

Key Responsibilities

  • Underwrite and analyze potential acquisitions using complex ARGUS and Excel cash flow models.
  • Build, maintain, and refine valuation models for assigned assets, reflecting new leases, market changes, and updated assumptions.
  • Coordinate quarterly valuations with asset managers, regional directors, and third‑party appraisers; review appraisals and reconcile issues.
  • Monitor property performance through monthly reporting, hold/sell analyses, and variance reviews.
  • Evaluate economic, demographic, and market data to support acquisition decisions and portfolio strategy.
  • Prepare clear, concise investment committee briefs and supporting materials.
  • Analyze lease transactions, including net effective rents, and assess value impact.
  • Assist with annual budgets, business plans, quarterly portfolio updates, and ad hoc analyses.
  • Support the due diligence process, including legal, financial, and physical reviews for new acquisitions.
  • Provide support on acquisition financing, including lender coordination.
  • Maintain and update the acquisition pipeline in Salesforce.
  • Participate in recurring leasing calls and property‑level strategy discussions.
  • Develop a deep understanding of assigned assets, markets, and tenant drivers.
  • Engage with brokers, developers, and operators to evaluate opportunities and validate underwriting assumptions.

Qualifications

  • 3–5 years of experience in real estate investment analysis, asset valuation, or a related financial discipline.
  • Bachelor’s degree required; business, finance, economics, or real estate focus preferred.
  • Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office; strong Excel modeling required.
  • Analytical rigor with the ability to break down complex data into clear insights.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills.
  • Highly organized, detail‑oriented, and able to manage multiple deadlines in a fast‑paced environment.
  • Ability to work independently while contributing effectively to a collaborative team.
  • Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to build relationships across internal and external stakeholders.
  • Resourceful problem‑solver with sound judgment.
  • Willingness to travel as needed.

jobs.title: Senior Asset Manager
jobs.description:
At Barings, we are as invested in our associates as we are in our clients. We recognize those who work diligently for us and reward them for personal and professional integrity, communication skills, distinct competencies and expertise in specific strategies, ability to collaborate as a team member and true dedication to the interests of our clients.

Barings is a leading global financial services firm dedicated to meeting the evolving investment and capital needs of our clients and customers. Through active asset management and direct origination, we provide innovative solutions and access to differentiated opportunities across public and private capital markets. A subsidiary of MassMutual, Barings maintains a strong global presence with business and investment professionals located across North America, Europe, and Asia Pacific.

Job Summary

The Senior Asset Manager will be based in the Melbourne Real Estate team and will report to the Senior Director, Major Projects. This position is an integral member of the Asset Management team, enthusiastically sharing their experience with others and assisting in the provision of a range of services to the business.  Working within a culture that thrives on teamwork, collaboration, performance outcomes and a passion for real estate, this senior manager will work to achieve optimum performance from a portfolio of commercial assets. The role will involve managing assets for Investors and Joint Venture partners. 

Primary Responsibilities

  • Lead and coordinate annually strategic asset planning process, focusing on each property’s key features and risks, performance, value add capital expenditure and customer strategy.
  • Informed by a strong understanding of the local and wider market conditions, competing assets, occupier business and an existing network of contacts in real estate, construction etc.
  • Demonstrate a proven track record in identifying value creation opportunities and executing strategies to realise such outcomes.
  • Demonstrate continuous improvement in process and compliance not limited to reporting, budgeting, valuation management, arrears management, WHS management, rent reviews, outgoings reconciliation and asset re-purposing.
  • Work closely with the Senior Director, Major Projects to review and negotiate significant leases and to resolve material issues where required.
  • Work with Property, Facilities Managers and other service providers in a way that they can enjoy clarity in understanding the asset goals, they experience inclusion and a sense of being an extension of our team, are inspired to ensure assets look and perform at their best.
  • Strengthen and enhance customer relationships to maximize customer satisfaction objectives and customer retention
  • In collaboration with the Barings ESG team, support and implement industry best practice sustainability initiatives across all aspects of the properties
  • Meet the legal and regulatory obligations of the Trustee and Manager of the respective Funds and Joint Venture agreements
  • Provide leadership, motivation and guidance to team members.
  • Comply with Barings procedures and probity requirements, anti-discrimination and Work Health and Safety legislation. Organise handover from Development Management team to Asset Management team and manage defects program.
  • Ensure effective communication lines are established and maintained with the Asset Team.
  • Demonstrate a collaborative and innovative approach and work with the Senior Director, Major Projects and team to deliver high quality project outcomes.
  • Meet project milestones for specific development projects, deliver project outcomes to a high professional standard, and demonstrate value for money and agreed financial returns to the business.

Qualifications

  • Qualification in property, finance or related field.
  • 15+ years' experience in an asset management role and a minimum of 7 years managing and/or leasing industrial assets in Melbourne.
  • Ability and track record to prepare strategic asset plans and clearly identify, articulate, and execute on those approved asset strategies.
  • Good market knowledge of commercial and industrial markets from an investor, leasing and capital transactions perspective.
  • Strong understanding of leasing process and valuation principles.
  • Knowledge of relevant Australian Acts, standards, codes and practices.
  • Ability to direct and lead outsourced property and facilities managers.
  • An understanding of customer experience, customer drivers and customer strategy execution.
  • A passion for ESG and implementing initiatives to enhance asset performance and occupier experience.
  • Proven customer engagement, dispute resolution and strategic relationship building skills.
  • Capability to understand and resolve complex property management, leasing and valuation issues.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to integrate quickly within a flexible team-oriented environment.
  • Proficiency with MS Office including Excel, Word, PowerPoint and budgeting tools.

#LI-GA1

Job Title: Real Estate Equity Acquisitions

Corporate Title: Associate Director

Department: Real Estate Equity

Location: Boston, MA

We are seeking a high‑impact Associate Director to join our Real Estate Acquisitions team and play a key role in evaluating and executing commercial real estate investments across the Eastern US. This individual will be a critical contributor to the full deal lifecycle—from initial underwriting through due diligence, structuring, and closing—while working closely with senior originators and investment committee leadership.

This is an opportunity to join a dynamic, growing platform with significant capital, strong market presence, and the ability to execute across the capital stack.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead the end‑to‑end execution of new acquisition opportunities across multiple property types.
  • Underwrite and analyze prospective investments using advanced ARGUS and Excel cash flow models.
  • Review offering materials, evaluate financial projections, and synthesize key risks and value drivers.
  • Maintain, enhance, and QC acquisition models to support consistent, high‑quality decision making.
  • Prepare clear, compelling investment briefs and materials for senior team members and the investment committee.
  • Present underwriting conclusions, market insights, and deal recommendations to senior acquisitions leaders.
  • Coordinate with brokers, developers, operators, and leasing teams to assess opportunities and validate assumptions.
  • Conduct deep market research—including economic, demographic, and real estate fundamentals—to support investment theses.
  • Drive the due diligence process, including legal, financial, and physical reviews, and support acquisition financing.
  • Collaborate closely with Acquisition Originators to support pipeline development and deal strategy.

Qualifications

  • 5–7 years of real estate investment analysis experience; direct deal execution experience strongly preferred.
  • Bachelor’s degree required, with emphasis in finance, business, economics, or real estate.
  • Advanced fluency in Microsoft Office (Excel modeling required); ARGUS proficiency is essential.
  • Strong quantitative and analytical foundation with the ability to distill complex information into clear insights.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with confidence presenting to senior stakeholders.
  • High level of organization, ownership, and attention to detail.
  • Ability to operate independently while thriving within a collaborative team environment.
  • Strong relationship‑building skills across internal teams and external counterparties.
  • Proven ability to manage multiple deadlines and operate in a fast‑paced, execution‑driven environment.
  • Willingness to travel as needed.

jobs.description:
