jobs.title: AVP,  ETF Capital Markets – Taiwan (Taiwan Based)
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for ourclients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What's in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including: 

  • Generous annual leave plus flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • Learning and development programs

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan (if applicable in your region)

Job Description

About the Role:

The AVP, Capital Markets will be responsible for supporting and expanding client‑facing capital markets engagement for Taiwan‑focused strategies, working closely with investment, trading, product, and distribution teams. This role will serve as a subject‑matter expert on Taiwan capital markets, acting as a key interface between investors, internal stakeholders, and market participants.

The specialist will support investor education, market insights, execution guidance, and product positioning, with a strong focus on local market structure, regulation, liquidity, and trading dynamics. The role will also contribute to broader APAC capital markets initiatives and data‑driven decision‑making.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead and support client and distributor engagement related to Taiwan capital markets, including market structure, liquidity, execution considerations, and regulatory developments.
  • Act as a trusted specialist for investors, articulating investment philosophy, process, performance, and market context for Taiwan‑focused products.
  • Provide market updates, capital markets insights, and execution guidance to internal stakeholders and external clients.
  • Partner closely with Portfolio Management, Trading, Operations, Legal/Compliance, Product, and Technology teams to support capital markets initiatives.
  • Produce quantitative analysis, market research, and trading analytics to support client discussions and internal decision‑making.
  • Collect, analyze, and interpret trading, flow, and liquidity data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities in the Taiwan market.
  • Contribute to capital markets strategy reporting, including development of tools, dashboards, and visualization to enhance understanding of investor behavior and market dynamics.
  • Support pre‑trade and post‑trade analytics, execution analysis, and reporting aligned with best‑execution objectives.
  • Write Taiwan capital markets insights, commentary, and research for internal and external audiences.
  • Collaborate with product development and research teams on new product launches and enhancements, ensuring alignment with local market requirements.

Experience & Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent with 5+ years of relevant experience in capital markets, trading, or investment roles with a strong focus on Taiwan markets.
  • Deep understanding of Taiwan capital markets, including market structure, trading mechanics, liquidity drivers, and investor behavior.
  • Strong knowledge of multi‑asset classes, including equities, fixed income, derivatives (futures, options, swaps), and FX.
  • Solid understanding of regulatory frameworks relevant to Taiwan (e.g., FSC, TWSE, TPEx) and ability to stay current on regulatory developments.
  • Strong quantitative and analytical skills, with experience producing market, trading, and performance analytics.
  • Proficiency in Bloomberg and market data tools.
  • Working knowledge of programming or data tools (e.g., Python, VBA, SQL) and experience with data visualization or automation is highly desirable.
  • Strong communication and relationship‑building skills, with the ability to explain complex investment and market concepts clearly to diverse audiences.
  • Comfortable operating in a fast‑paced, dynamic environment, managing multiple initiatives simultaneously.
  • Self‑directed, proactive, and able to independently drive initiatives from concept to execution.
  • High learning agility and strong problem‑solving skills, with a mindset focused on continuous improvement and business impact.

How to Apply:

  • Apply for the role directly here or atInvesco Careers:Careers at Invesco | APAC.

To know more about us:

  • About our CR program:https://www.invesco.com/corporate/en/our-commitments/corporate-responsibility.html

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model


At Invesco, our workplace model supports our culture and meets the needs of our clients while providing flexibility our employees value. As a full-time employee, compliance with the workplace policy means working with your direct manager to create a schedule where you will work in your designated office at least four days a week, with one day working outside an Invesco office.  

Invesco's culture of inclusivity and its commitment to diversity in the workplace are demonstrated through our people practices. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, creed, color, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, national origin, citizenship status, disability, age, or veteran status. Our equal opportunity employment efforts comply with all applicable local laws governing non-discrimination in employment.

Personal data provided by job applicants will be used strictly in accordance with our personal data policy and for recruitment purposes only. Candidates not notified within twelve weeks may consider their application unsuccessful. All related information will be kept in our file for future recruitment purposes. When there are vacancies in our subsidiary or associate companies, we may transfer the application to them for consideration of employment. Under the Personal Data (Privacy) Ordinance, job applicants have a right to request access to, and to request correction of, Personal Data in relation to the application. If job applicants wish to exercise these rights, they should contact the Human Resources department.

jobs.title: Senior Associate, Private Markets Product – US Wealth, Real Estate
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

The Senior Associate will join Invesco’s Private Markets Product – US Wealth, Real Estate team, with primary responsibility for developing and delivering clear, compelling marketing and messaging for private real estate strategies designed for the U.S. wealth channel.

While the role is part of the broader Invesco Private Markets platform, the core of the responsibilities will be focused on how private real estate strategies are positioned, articulated, and visually presented to financial advisors and investors. This includes translating complex private fund structures, portfolio construction concepts, and investment strategies into high-quality, accessible materials across presentations, written content, email, and digital formats. The role will also have the opportunity to get involved in other aspects of Product such as product strategy and product development.

The role requires strong editorial judgment, visual sensibility, and a solid understanding of private markets, with the ability to speak intelligently about different private markets asset classes and strategies and clearly position private real estate within a broader private markets allocation and within retail and wealth management investment portfolios more broadly.

Key Responsibilities / Duties:

  • Develop and refine core messaging and positioning for private real estate strategies offered through the wealth channel.

  • Translate complex private real estate investment concepts into clear, advisor- and investor-friendly narratives.

  • Partner with Marketing to ensure high-quality visual presentation and effective storytelling.

  • Create and maintain presentations, commentaries, FAQs, talking points, and digital materials.

  • Articulate how private real estate compares with and complements other private markets asset classes such as private equity and private credit.

  • Collaborate with Investment Teams, Client PMs, Sales, Compliance, Legal, and Operations.

  • Incorporate feedback from Sales and clients into iterative improvements to messaging and materials.

Work Experience / Knowledge:

  • 4-6 years of experience in financial services with a focus on marketing and communications.

  • Familiarity with private markets, private fund structures, or the financial advisor market is preferred.

  • Strong familiarity with private markets, private fund structures, and the financial advisor market is strongly preferred.

  • Experience or exposure to private real estate is a plus but not required.

  • Ability to communicate differences across private markets asset classes.

Skills / Other Personal Attributes Required:

  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills with strong editorial judgment.

  • Strong PowerPoint skills and visual sensibility.

  • Highly organized, proactive, and collaborative.

Formal Education:

  • Undergraduate degree

The total compensation offered for this position, which includes salary and incentive pay, will vary based on skills, experience and location. The salary range for this position in New York City is $130K to $150K annually.

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model

Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities and qualifications required of employees assigned to this job. The job holder may be required to perform other duties as deemed appropriate by their manager from time to time.

jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution

Job Description

The general role of the Senior Associate is to work with the regional Origination Officers, and the Debt Capital Markets team to underwrite all new debt transactions entering IRE’s pipeline and manage the process throughout the various stages of the due diligence and syndication process.

Key Responsibilities / Duties:

  • Valuation analysis of potential originations of institutional quality multi-family, industrial, office, hospitality, and specialty asset class properties.

  • Lead the creation of thorough and concise investment packages that are presented to the Credit Investment Committee, performing and providing all support for assumptions utilized during the process.

  • Effectively identify and articulate the major risks associated with an investment opportunity in both verbal and written format.

  • Perform and provide expertise on all aspects of the financial modeling, focusing on property level analysis of institutional real estate, as well as loan cash flows, structure and credit metrics.  Verifiable Excel modeling expertise is a requirement.

  • Research and formulate corporate tenant credit analysis using multiple resources including financial statements and other online financial services

Work Experience / Knowledge:

  • Minimum of 4 years of experience in the underwriting of debt or equity investments in commercial real estate

  • Must be a power user of Argus and Excel, with skill level verified through an externally administered exam a significant advantage

  • Preference will be given to candidates with experience underwriting properties in the Western U.S. and/or with experience in debt syndications

Skills / Other Personal Attributes Required:

  • Strong interpersonal, communication (speaking and writing) and listening skills.

  • Strong quantitative analysis skills

  • Ability to prioritize, organize, time manage, balance competing deadlines, and be a self-starter are essential

Formal Education:

  • Undergraduate degree is required, advanced degree or applicable experience a plus

This position will be located in our Los Angeles office: 10250 Constellation Blvd, Los Angeles 90067

The total compensation offered for this position, which includes salary and incentive pay, will vary based on skills, experience and location. The salary range for this position in Los Angeles is $125,000 to $145,000 annually.

Job Description

The Role

The MD Global Liquidity Direct Sales will source, develop, and expand direct institutional client relationships across the United States with a primary focus on growing assets in the firm’s money market funds. You will work closely with the Head of Global Liquidity Relationship Management and senior U.S. distribution leaders to align strategic goals and execute on national growth initiatives.

This role partners extensively with portfolio managers, product teams, and the broader U.S. institutional sales organization to deliver cash‑management insights, drive enterprise engagement, and support the firm’s liquidity strategy across corporations, public entities, nonprofits, and other institutional investors.

Responsibilities of the role:

  • Drive growth of U.S. money market fund assets through direct engagement with corporate treasurers, finance leaders, institutional investors, and public sector clients.
  • Increase market share and brand visibility within the U.S. institutional liquidity marketplace.
  • Integrate Global Liquidity priorities with the firm’s national institutional distribution strategy.
  • Partner with Global Portfolio Managers to communicate market conditions, client insights, and evolving product needs.
  • Build and maintain senior‑level relationships across treasury, finance, and investment organizations.
  • Represent the firm through client presentations and participation in U.S. industry conferences, treasury events, and client forums.
  • Collaborate with product, legal, marketing, operations, and compliance teams to support effective business execution and client outcomes.
  • Leverage data, analytics, and CRM discipline to prioritize high‑value opportunities, ensure accurate activity tracking, and support future growth.

Requirements of the role:

  • 10+ years of direct sales experience in the U.S. institutional market, preferably within cash management or liquidity solutions.
  • Deep understanding of money market funds, institutional distribution, and corporate treasury needs within the U.S. investment landscape.
  • Proven track record of raising and retaining institutional assets and consistently meeting or exceeding sales targets.
  • Strong knowledge of pricing, liquidity vehicles, operational workflows, and relationship management in a regulated environment.
  • Ability to anticipate and articulate market trends, regulatory developments, and product changes impacting liquidity management.
  • Strategic, commercially focused mindset with experience operating effectively in a large, global organization.
  • Exceptional communication, presentation, and relationship‑building skills, including engagement with C‑suite and senior treasury leaders.
  • Bachelor’s degree required; MBA or advanced degree preferred; FINRA registrations required (7, 63) and relevant certifications (e.g., CTP, CFA, CFP) a plus.

The salary range for this position is $160,000 to $185,000/ year. The total compensation offered for this position includes salary and incentive pay and will vary based on skills, experience and location.

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

jobs.title: Senior Director, Credit Asset Management (Los Angeles Office)
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

The Senior Director, Credit Asset Management will be responsible for multiple diverse portfolios of commercial real estate loans originated and held by Invesco’s growing commercial real estate lending business line. The role will include oversight of third-party asset managers and leadership of loan modifications, complex workouts and resolutions.

Key Responsibilities / Duties:

  • Manage third party asset management team comprising 15 dedicated employees of Bellwether Asset Management.  Establish standards, responsibilities and expectations.  Monitor team performance and oversee work product to ensure consistency with Invesco standards.

  • Train third party asset management team and ensure accountability to Invesco standards.

  • Actively evaluate performance of individual asset managers and recommend hires, promotions, performance improvement plans, and terminations.

  • Ensure completion of third party scope of work within specified deadlines.  Evaluate additional scope of work as necessary. Negotiate third party contracts.

  • Lead communication and delegation of responsibilities between Invesco in-house credit team and third party asset management team.

  • Proactively monitor portfolio level performance, with a focus on Watchlist, sub-performing and non-performing loans.

  • Review third party work product and assign ad hoc tasks.

  • Oversee all loan asset management matters including payoffs, prepayments, lease reviews/approvals, SNDAs, reserves, escrows, draw requests, cash management, easements, financial reporting, post-closing items, waiver, modifications, valuation process and borrower requests.

  •   Lead the execution of loss-minimizing recovery strategies such as note sales, workouts, restructures, and/or foreclosure.

  • Collaborate with internal loan originators and accounting team, as well as third-party servicer(s), capital markets team, lenders and legal counsel to drive optimal outcomes for Watchlist, sub-performing and non-performing loans.

  • Lead risk rating process, identify problem / watchlist loans, develop monitoring plans, and recommendations.

  • Lead monthly and quarterly loan valuations processes by managing external and internal valuations professionals and external loan asset managers with valuation oversight responsibilities.  Proactively communicate with Portfolio Management team.

  • Identify and implement process efficiencies, best practices and/or standard operating procedures.

Work Experience / Knowledge:

  • 15+ years of commercial loan asset management or underwriting experience preferred.

  • Construction loan, mortgage workout and senior/sub (ie mezzanine) experience is strongly preferred.

  • Strong management skills with attention to detail and ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.

  • Ability to manage and collaborate with multiple external groups (third party asset manager, third party servicer) and internal groups (portfolio management, valuations, underwriting, origination)

  • Demonstrated expertise in workouts and special situations.

  • Highly proficient user of Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, and Word.

  • Proficient quantitative analysis, research, and problem-solving capabilities.

Skills / Other Personal Attributes Required:

  • Self-motivated and team oriented, with strong time management skills to prioritize deadlines.

  • Collaborative approach.

  • Excellent analytical, verbal and written communication skills. Ability to distill concepts and present results.

  • Confident individual ability to provide checks and balances within internal and external groups

  • Strong written and verbal communication skills.

Formal Education:

  • Bachelor’s degree in finance or related field.

  • MBA strongly preferred.

Working Conditions:

  • 5 days in-office in Century City, California.

  • 25% travel

The total compensation offered for this position, which includes salary and incentive pay, will vary based on skills, experience and location. The salary range for this position in Los Angeles is $180,000 to $250,000 annually.

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model

Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution

Job Description

About the Team:

To build solutions that better automate tasks within the Investment Risk organization. The Investment Risk team operates independently to provide objective oversight to senior executives, to portfolio management teams, and to external parties such as Fund boards.  Investment Risk identifies, measures, and manages a wide array of investment-related risks across all portfolios using advanced analytics and qualitative methods.  Investment Risk focuses on portfolio construction to raise awareness of unintended risks that may detract from strategy objectives.

About the Role: This Quantitative Engineer role sits within the Strategy & Execution team, which serves an important function within the Investment Risk group.  Strategy & Execution drives change management, manages projects with enterprise leaders, and builds software to help strengthen the impact of the Investment Risk team.  This role will help redefine the standard of automated solutions used to support the team’s mission, ensuring seamless risk monitoring and alignment with organizational frameworks and objectives.

Requirements of the Role:

  • Degree in a quantitative subject such as Statistics, Economics, Financial Engineering, Computer Science

  • CFA, FRM/PRM and/or MBA (Finance) are desirable

  • Experience with AI and scripting applications used to enhance efficiency

  • Experience as a team player helping to contribute to technology project deliverables

  • Experience working in cloud-based environments such as AWS is a plus

  • Experience in applying machine learning techniques is a plus

  • Not mandatory, but it would be helpful to have previous experience with vendor software commonly used in asset management: Barra, RiskMetrics, FactSet, Charles River, Aladdin, Bloomberg, Morningstar, Macrobond, Compustat, Worldscope, DataStream

  • Intermediate coding skills with extensive hands-on experience in R, Python, SQL or other languages used to engineer large datasets

  • Good understanding of financial instruments and a familiarity with international financial markets (e.g.- ., Equities, Fixed Income, FX, Derivatives)

  • Fundamental understanding of core machine learning concepts is preferred

  • Effectively operate within a team-oriented and collaborative environment

  • Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to build relationships across all levels of an organization and to drive stakeholder consensus

  • Intellectually curious with attention to detail

  • Superior problem-solving capability

  • Highly motivated self-starter with ability to operate autonomously and independently

  • Comfort working at the intersection of business, data, and technology, translating needs into practical solutions

  • Ability to communicate process and automation concepts clearly to both technical and non‑technical stakeholders

  • Good time management and able to work under occasional time- pressure

  • Comfortable working within a project management team and managing multiple concurrent deliverables

jobs.title: Quantitative Analyst, Portfolio Engineering & Analytics
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

About the Team:

Responsible for developing and systematizing multi-asset investment frameworks for our solutions orientated clients, as part of the Invesco Solutions group which manages $210 billion* in assets across several systematic and quantitative strategies. The role involves close collaboration with defined benefit and insurance entities, as well as defined contribution plans and wealth entities. Support strategic asset allocation, portfolio construction and advisory, leveraging latest cloud, optimization, and visualization technologies. Responsible for Invesco’s flagship cloud-based and client facing, portfolio construction and analytics platform – Invesco Vision®.

(*As of September 30, 2025)

About the Role:

As part of the portfolio engineering & analytics team, you will work alongside some of the best quantitative researchers and technologists to help evolve our investment capabilities.  You will be responsible for performing research, managing data, and developing APIs that will be used to power the investment process along with our flagship portfolio construction and analytics platform – Invesco Vision®. The role will allow for significant opportunities for creativity and innovation.

Responsibilities of the Role:

  • Helping construct multi-asset and single asset portfolios using advanced asset allocation and portfolio construction techniques

  • Building high performance computing algorithms, infrastructure and APIs

  • Researching and building prediction and forecasting methods based on both classical statistical techniques as well as ML based techniques including Agentic workflows leveraging Generative AI

  • Helping translate investment frameworks from various segments of the market (i.e. cash flow driven investing, liability driven investing, insurance capital efficient portfolio construction) into tangible investment and client engagement tools

  • Leveraging advanced optimization techniques to create optimal portfolio solutions for internal and external stakeholders

  • Employing multi-period simulation tools to project and optimize performance in the context of flows and optionality

  • Integrating third party risk models in various portfolio construction exercises

  • Designing and maintaining procedures and tools that make data management and research more efficient

  • Working closely with other quantitative and technology teams in the firm in leveraging best practices from a financial theory and technological perspective.

  • Formulating new ideas for research that will help enhance frameworks and tools

  • Following academic research and industry trends to constantly incorporate best practice

Requirements of the Role:

  • Advanced degree in quantitative disciplines such as engineering, finance, operations research, or computer science is required
  • Solid understanding of standard financial engineering techniques
  • Excellent knowledge of statistics and optimization
  • Strong Python programming expertise, including experience with FastAPI for building RESTful services, Numba for high-performance computing, and deploying solutions on cloud environments (AWS, Azure, or GCP)
  • Strong background in technical infrastructure development, Git, and cloud technologies is advantageous
  • Researching, implementing, and integrating Large Language Models (LLMs) and conversational AI technologies to develop intelligent client engagement tools and chatbots will be beneficial
  • Experience managing and manipulating large data sets (preferably in SQL)
  • Strong ability to learn and translate abstract principles into systematic algorithmic representations
  • Some experience using third party risk models such as BarraOne or Axioma will be a plus
  • Progress towards CFA designation preferred
The salary range for this position in Boston and New York City is $120,000 - $135,000 / year. The total compensation offered for this position includes salary and incentive pay and will vary based on skills, experience and location.

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model

Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

jobs.title: Senior Analyst, Asia & Emerging Markets
jobs.description:
As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

Job Description

We have an outstanding benefits package which includes:

  • Company-provided healthcare 

  • A competitive annual leave allowance 

  • Flexible working options, including hybrid working arrangements 

  • Generous pension provisions 

  • Income protection 

  • Health and wellness benefits 

  • Volunteering days 

  • Enhanced parental leave 

  • Life insurance 

About the Role:

We are looking for a Senior Analyst to join Invesco’s Fundamental Equities team. This role is central to generating differentiated investment insights and supporting long‑term, conviction‑based portfolios. You will be responsible for fundamental equity analysis of companies across sectors & geographies as directed by the Invesco Asia and Emerging Markets team. As a senior member of the team, you will bring intellectual curiosity, independent thinking, and commercial awareness—while contributing to a collaborative, high‑challenge investment culture.

Key Responsibilities:

Fundamental Research & Analysis

  • Conduct in‑depth quantitative and qualitative company analysis, including modelling, valuation, and assessment of issuer fundamentals across a range of industries

  • Build, maintain, and flex financial models to test investment scenarios and support decision‑making

  • Integrate qualitative insights—such as competitive positioning, value chains, and industry dynamics—into company valuations

  • Maintain a strong understanding of macro, political, economic, sector, and industry factors and incorporate these into research conclusions

Idea Generation & Investment Insight

  • Generate and develop new investment ideas aligned to the team’s philosophy and strategies

  • Identify and prioritise key research focus areas based on company context and portfolio needs

  • Assess forward‑looking risks and opportunities by distilling complex information into clear, fact‑based insights

Communication & Stakeholder Engagement

  • Present investment ideas clearly and persuasively, both verbally and in written form

  • Lead company meetings, engaging effectively with senior management and drawing out material insights

  • Represent the investment team with clients and commercial partners, explaining performance, outlook, and portfolio contributions

  • Build and maintain trusted relationships with company management, brokers, analysts, and industry contacts

Systems & Tools

  • Use investment and data platforms effectively, including Bloomberg, FactSet, research management systems, and Microsoft Office applications

What We’re Looking For:

  • Significant experience in fundamental equity research, investment analysis, or a related buy‑side or sell‑side role

  • Strong financial modelling, valuation, and accounting skills

  • Proven ability to generate and articulate differentiated investment ideas

  • Confidence engaging with senior company management and external stakeholders

  • Intellectual curiosity, commercial awareness, and sound judgment

  • A collaborative mindset aligned with a high‑performance investment culture

  • Educated to degree level or equivalent. On a path to, or completed, additional qualifications that would allow you to undertake fiduciary duties in due course (i.e. IMC, CFA, etc.)

In addition to technical excellence, success in this role requires strong professional judgment and leadership behaviours:

  • Lead upwards by providing thoughtful, evidence‑based input that improves decisions and processes

  • Seek and provide constructive challenge, using debate to strengthen research and outcomes

  • Communicate with clarity and credibility, tailoring messages to different audiences and fostering open dialogue

  • Demonstrate independent thinking, resilience, and courage, constructively challenging the status quo when supported by evidence

  • Uphold the highest ethical, legal, regulatory, and fiduciary standards, contributing to a strong compliance culture

  • Promote innovation and continuous improvement, adapting to change and investing in ongoing development

  • Value and encourage diverse perspectives, creating an inclusive environment where different viewpoints are welcomed

  • Mentor and support other analysts, sharing knowledge and providing constructive feedback

Disability Confident Scheme: 

Applicants who opt in to the Disability Confident Scheme and meet the criteria for the role will be offered an interview. We are committed to providing an inclusive recruitment process for all candidates who make an application. By opting-in to this scheme, applicants will be disclosing that they have a disability solely for the purpose of the Disability Confident Scheme.   

The Disability Confident Scheme only guarantees an interview – it does not automatically mean that applicants interviewed will gain employment with Invesco at that time.  

To apply through the Disability Confident Scheme, you’ll need to firstly ensure you have applied for the role via our external careers page. Following this, you’ll need to email us at emea-talentacquisition@invesco.comconfirming your wish to opt-in, alongside your contact details and the title of the role you wish to apply for.  

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

No

Workplace Model


Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

If this sounds like you, we’d love to hear from you! We want all of our candidates to shine during the application and selection process, so if you need any adjustments to be made, please send an e-mail to emea-talentacquisition@invesco.com. Please include your name, the job you are interested in, and the type of adjustment you need (for example; breaks during your interview, remote interviews, additional time for assessments or other required adjustments)

We promote a working environment that welcomes everyone and creates inclusive teams, celebrates difference and encourages everyone to be themselves at work.

Our commitment to the community and environmental, social and governance investing:
We partner with charitable organisations globally to make an impact in the communities where we live and work. Our people are encouraged to support the charities they feel most passionate about.  We are also committed to environmental, social and governance (ESG) investing. We serve our clients in this space as a trusted partner both on specific responsible investment product strategies as well as part of our commitment to deliver a superior investment experience. 

Recruitment Agencies: 
Invesco has an in-house recruitment team, which focuses on sourcing great candidates directly. Invesco will not accept unsolicited resumes from agency or search firm recruiters. Fees will not be paid in the event a candidate submitted by a recruiter without an agreement in place is hired. When we do use agencies, we have a PSL in place, so please do not contact hiring managers directly.

Regulatory:
This position may fall in-scope of one or multiple regimes/directives.

jobs.title: Quantitative Analyst
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

The Team


This role is part of the Systematic Investing team within Invesco Fixed Income.  The team is responsible for managing over $40 billion in assets under management across an array of fixed income products including credit, interest rates and foreign currencies.  The team manages exchange traded funds (ETF’s), mutual funds and separately managed accounts with operations and clients spanning the US, Europe and Asia.

The Role


In this role, you will collaborate closely with portfolio managers and quantitative analysts to build software that powers quantitative investment processes supporting the systematic fixed income platform. The primary focus will be software engineering and development—designing and implementing tools and models that directly support portfolio management and investment decision-making.  The work will play a critical role in automating portfolio management tasks and enhancing the efficiency and effectiveness of our investment strategies.  You will gain broad exposure to the investment lifecycle, including research, portfolio construction, trading, and attribution.  The impact of your contributions will be tangible, influencing investment decisions and fund performance. 

Key Responsibilities:
•          Developing software to synthesize quantitative models with fundamental analysis to implement investment strategies with superior risk adjusted returns

•          Provides support to senior portfolio managers to help drive the development of our research IP related to fixed income investing

•          Performs maintenance and improve existing infrastructure for all our production process

•          Researches cyclical and structural drivers of global economic performance using statistical and fundamental analysis.

•          Communicates the results of economic and market models across the platform.

•          Examines and communicates analysis of quantitative models developed by third parties to assess potential value and usage in the team’s overall quantitative approach.

•          Interacts with other Invesco quantitative groups for insights and potential synergies across groups.

•          Serve as quantitative resource to broader IFI platform for quantitative analysis needs.

Work Experience / Knowledge:
•          1-5 years’ experience developing software supporting investment processes is required.  A focus on fixed income investments is a plus but not required.

•          1-5 years quantitative research experience is required.  A focus on fixed income investments is a plus but not required.

•          Experience in automating investment workflows, including data processing, portfolio construction, and performance analysis

•          Experience with version control (Git), CI/CD, and Unix/Linux environments

•          Knowledge of the principles of investment analysis, statistics as would be acquired through the completion of a Bachelor’s degree in Quantitative Finance or related field is required.

•          Strong grasp of data engineering fundamentals and working with financial datasets

Skills / Other Personal Attributes Required:

•          Strong software engineering skills building scalable, production quality systems for investment teams

•          Proficiency in R or Python and SQL for data science and application development

•          Familiarity with AI coding tools (e.g. AI agents, MCP, context aware tooling)

•          Ability to assimilate processes from three key roles in Investment Management: portfolio management, analyst and trader

•          Strong ability to communicate with internal and external parties.

•          Ability to gather and interpret market data from various sources such as third party and in-house databases.

•          Ability to prioritize work, meet deadlines, and concentrate on details in a fast-paced work environment.

•          The ability to collaborate in a team environment is critical.

Formal Education:

•          Bachelor’s Degree in quantitative finance or equivalent required.

•          A Master’s degree or a CFA designation is a plus but not required.

jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for ourclients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What's in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including: 

  • Generous annual leave plus flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • Learning and development programs

Job Description

About the Role:

The primary role of Senior Manager/Associate Director is responsible to develop and close new business, sales generation in Hong Kong and Macau for complex institutional and pension accounts.

This will involve developing new leads, preparing RFPs, giving presentations to prospective clients, ensuring client and prospects receive full and efficient service and promotes products managed by our office and to extend to other offices within the Group.

Looking for highly motivated candidate with solid industry connection as well as knowledge, who will be able to identify and source deals and to handle the entire Sales process.

Responsibilities of the role:

  • Develop new Institutional & Pension business opportunities in Hong Kong and Macau with key prospects, clients and to liaise with related consultants. 
  • Identify potential clients, to manage preparation of presentations, to follow up with the client contacts from sales of investment services to deal close.
  • Evaluate on detailed analysis such as asset liabilities, data research and analysis, investment risk return profile, etc. in particular for pension and insurance clients.
  • Develop in-depth knowledge of Institutional clients, understand their business needs and to effectively assist them in decision making process.
  • Partner with regional and global investment centers to shape the core offerings (traditional long-only strategies, alternatives, multi-assets, insurance solutions, passively managed instruments & indexing capabilities) to cater for different client needs to maximize growth.
  • Generate ideas and tactics, and to coordinate with RFP team to develop innovative responses for the deals.
  • Contribute ideas for new products which will be suitable to the markets and business segments assigned.
  • Achieve activity statistics for the region.
  • Lead or participate in other ad-hoc projects as assigned.
  • Maintains good relationship with all internal stakeholders whilst to understand and to cater for client needs.
  • Contribution in helping the team to reach the leadership position objective with the right optimal approach to meet the needs of Invesco as well as to the clienteles.
  • Ensure that procedures and compliance requirements are compiled.

Requirement for the Role: 

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent – preferably in Finance or Investments related.
  • Relevant professional licenses of the Asset Management industry.
  • At least 6+ years of total relevant working experience
  • Deep knowledge and experience of Institutional clients in Hong Kong and Macau.
  • Strong experience within Insurance segment is a plus
  • Able to think strategically and out-of-the-box
  • Creative in coming up with new ideas in response to prospects’ needs
  • Experience in servicing clients and dealing with sophisticated institutional clients at a senior level
  • Good time management skills
  • Well organized and results focused
  • Ability to multi-task across strategic and tactical work tasks
  • Accurate, articulate, presentable and showing initiative

jobs.title: Associate, Investment Management
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

The Associate partners with Asset Managers in the management and oversight of various commercial and multifamily real estate investments, with the overall goal of optimizing asset performance and values. The Associate plays an integral role in developing and executing property specific strategies, analyzing major leasing decisions and how they impact value, performing major feasibility studies and evaluating property level performance against relevant industry benchmarks. In addition, the role is responsible for providing support and analysis for the preparation of the quarterly and annual property reports and budgets for a portfolio of assets.

SPECIFIC DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Work with Asset Manager to create property level operational strategies and analyze major leases, capital projects or other investment factors impacting asset value.

  • Assist in the review of valuation models (Argus and Excel) and take part in the acquisition and disposition process, overseeing the flow of due diligence information for select investments as needed.

  • Prepare financial analysis to track historic portfolio and property level results in comparison to industry benchmarks.

  • Coordinate the development of Annual Business Plans and Quarterly reports by assisting in the preparation of property operating and capital budgets and reviewing and approving on-going financial variance reporting.   

  • Communicate with third party property management and leasing personnel as well as insurance and real estate tax consultants and other third party providers as needed.

  • Review lease submittal packages, evaluate prospective tenant businesses and credit worthiness, and perform other lease related financial analysis as needed.

  • Collect, analyze and synthesize relevant market reports and other lease or transaction related data.  

  • Help facilitate lender compliance with asset specific requirements such as obtaining approvals for lease agreements or significant capital expenditure projects and coordinating the release of escrow accounts.

  • Support enforcement of internal audit requirements with third party providers, and further ensure that such firms are conducting business in accordance with property management agreements.

POSITION QUALIFICATIONS

Education:

  • Four year or equivalent undergraduate degree in Business or related field. Post graduate education a plus.  A commitment to continued education in real estate will be encouraged.

Work Experience:

  • 2 – 5 years of experience in the commercial and/or multi-family real estate industry (or business related activity) is required.

  • Property level experience is a plus.

Skills:

  • Effective interpersonal, communication (verbal and written) and listening skills.

  • Strong computer literacy including fluency with Argus, VTS, RealPage, Microsoft Excel and Word, and PowerPoint.

  • Ability to understand and interpret commercial lease agreements and language as well as present value concepts and financial terminology.

  • Analytical problem solving skills capable to undertake detailed analyses and make well-reasoned, decisive recommendations.

  • Demonstrated prioritization, organization and time management skills necessary to balance competing deadlines.

  • Desire to multi-task in a complex, high-volume environment and competency to work with all levels of management and personnel on critical projects.

Travel:

  • Some travel will be required for periodic asset management related property visits and select acquisition due diligence

#MTIVZ

The total compensation offered for this position, which includes salary and incentive pay, will vary based on skills, experience and location. The salary range for this position in New York City is $110,000 to $120,000 annually.

This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

jobs.title: Senior Analyst, Asset-Backed Investments
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

The Invesco Structured Investments team manages a wide range of strategies to support the broader Invesco Fixed Income platform. Its mandate spans the residential and commercial agency and non-agency mortgage-backed sectors as well as the asset-backed securities sector with the goal of delivering best-in-class fund performance. The team manages approximately $40 bn USD across a variety of strategies including mutual funds, ETFs, institutional separately managed accounts, and an Agency-focused mortgage REIT. 

The Senior Analyst will serve as a key member of the team responsible for evaluating, selecting and positioning public and private asset-backed investments across Invesco-managed products. They will also participate in business development activities with both current and prospective clients. Additionally, the Senior Analyst will be actively involved in elevating the team profile across the broader Invesco Fixed Income platform.

Responsibilities:

  • Collaborate with portfolio managers and other analysts on a variety of public and private asset-backed finance investment strategies managed by the Structured Investments team, including mutual funds, exchange-traded funds and institutional accounts

  • Deep experience in asset-backed underwriting which includes, but is not limited to, assessing underlying collateral and conducting loan and bond cash flow modeling

  • Underwrite public ABS and private asset-backed finance investments across a wide range of ABS/ABF sectors and issuers. The team invests in flow, liquid credit, esoteric, and non-public sectors

  • Conduct market research and assist senior team members in formulating an investment outlook

  • Utilize excel, general-purpose programming languages and artificial intelligence to conduct monthly credit surveillance/monitoring

  • Employ a relative value-oriented mindset when assessing cross-sector asset allocation opportunities across the universe of public and private structured products (ABS, CMBS, MBS, asset-backed loans and mortgage-backed loans) as well as other fixed income sectors

  • Develop meaningful relationships with loan originators and sell-side contacts to help facilitate deal flow and best execution on investment acquisitions

  • Interact with broader Structured Investments analysts to better understand underwriting, internal rating processes and sector nuances

  • Formulate and communicate trade ideas for the broader Invesco Fixed Income platform to generate potential alpha for the wider set of investment teams

  • Periodically interact with current or prospective retail and institutional investors

  • Draft periodic investment commentary for both internal and external audiences

  • Commercialize asset-backed investment capabilities through product development and marketing efforts

Qualifications:

  • 7+ years of experience in underwriting asset-backed securities or private asset-backed finance investments across a variety of collateral types (e.g., credit cards, autos, student loans, whole business, aircraft, container, data center, fiber optic, etc.)

  • Expertise in the use of Intex and Bloomberg

  • Strong knowledge of Microsoft Office, including Excel and PowerPoint

  • Python and VBA programming skills are a plus

  • CFA Charterholder or pursuit thereof preferred   

Preferred Skills & Attributes:

  • Humble, client centric focus - we are fiduciaries of our clients’ assets and must understand their goals, desires, and investment objectives

  • Commercially oriented mindset, with desire to assist in growth of the ABS and private asset-backed finance platform

  • Open minded and willingness to learn new topics and investment/asset subsectors

  • Strong written and verbal presentation skills and ability to work constructively as a key member of a dynamic investment team

  • Highly methodical with a strong attention to detail

  • Proficiency in quantitative methods and analytical mindset

  • Ability to articulate a comprehensive investment framework and adapt it to meet specific client objectives

  • Ability to source investments and drive outcomes through decisive risk-taking

This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

About the Role:

The Associate Quantitative Analyst, Fixed Income will be the primary developer and maintainer of internal systems critical to the daily management of our successful and growing Fixed Income ETF lineup. The Analyst will work under the direction of ETF Portfolio Management to operate and extend tools for automated trading, performance attribution, corporate action analysis, basket negotiation tools and data quality monitoring, among other duties. Successful candidates will be self-directed, curious and eager to identify and implement opportunities to improve the portfolio management process. Knowledge of Python and SQL is required. The ability to work in a group environment with daily interactions with portfolio managers, information technology, and operational staff is expected. General knowledge of ETFs, currency hedging, fixed income securities, trading strategies, broker relationships and portfolio management functions is a plus, but not required. The position, located in Downers Grove, Illinois, will serve as a single contributor with no immediate direct reports. 

Responsibilities of the Role:

  • Develop, support and maintain our proprietary risk, flow and order optimization/monitoring system.
  • Own our In-Kind Order negotiation and capture process, working directly with Brokers, Portfolio Management, Trading and Technology to enhance our capabilities.
  • Develop a full understanding of our investment process; become capable of handling orders and rebalances with minimal supervision.
  • Gain a thorough understanding of the global ETF infrastructure and workflow, which includes Portfolio Management requirements for ETF portfolios managed in Europe, Asia, and the United States.

Requirements of the Role:

  • Bachelor's degree in computer science, physics, mathematics, or electrical engineering, or other scientific discipline preferred.
  • Prior experience with Python and SQL is required. Experience with JavaScript (esp. React/Angular), Rust, C, C++, Java, C# or similar is also valuable.
  • Experience with Polars, Plotly Dash (or equivalent), Flask, and the standard python data science stack.
  • Basic knowledge of git is required. Experience with CI tools (GitHub/BitBucket pipelines, Jenkins) is a plus.
  • Working knowledge of Linear Programming at the undergraduate level is desirable. Experience with libraries such as Gurobi/CPLEX/SCIP is a huge plus.
  • Linux experience, or experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure, etc.) is a plus.
  • Fixed Income/Trading experience is desirable but not required.
  • Ability to make quick decisions, establish priorities and concentrate on detailed information under time sensitive conditions.
  • Knowledge of the global fixed income markets, prior trading experience, and the ability to provide market color are preferred skills but not required.
  • Proficiency in any of the following PM systems is considered a plus: Aladdin, Bloomberg, Charles River.

The salary range for this position in Downers Grove is $125,000 to $147,000 annually. The total compensation offered for this position, which includes salary and incentive pay, will vary based on skills, experience and location.

Your Role:

To ensure successful delivery of new private markets product launches and product change initiatives with an emphasis on technical, structural, regulatory, tax, customer channels, disclosures and operational items, whilst managing internal governance and key stakeholder relationships.

What you will be doing:

  • To independent project manage private market product initiatives from design and approval through to implementation in conjunction with all key business partners.

  • To contribute across all phases of the Product Development Lifecycle with an emphasis on technical, structural, regulatory, tax, customer channels, disclosures and operational business case inputs.

  • Manage internal governance as required (adhering to the requirements of various committees, boards, product development lifecycle and policies and procedures).

  • To successfully manage any issues and risks relating to each product initiative.

  • Relationship building - Develop close working relationships with key stakeholders both within the business (e.g. Investments, Distribution, Control Functions & Operations) and within key counterparts to resolve barriers to the successful achievement of agreed projects.

    Prior experience with Private Markets product structuring and/or product management

  • Understanding of fund structures, governance, and regulatory frameworks.

  • In depth knowledge of financial markets with investment experience a plus

  • Proficient in Microsoft Office applications, including Word, Excel, & PowerPoint

  • Familiar with third party data providers

  • Proven communication and interpersonal skills

  • PRINCE2, Agile or another project management accreditation desirable but not essential

  • Educated to Master’s degree level or equivalent from recognised university and/or CFA would be preferable, ideally with finance, mathematics, engineering or economic qualifications

  • Strategic thinking skills

  • Commercial/ business acumen

  • Written communication and oral presentation skills

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for ourclients. Job Description

About the role:

Reporting to Team Leader, Hong Kong & China, the successful candidate will become responsible for managing Greater China/Chinese equities funds investing in Greater China/Chinese equities. This role is expected to conduct own fundamental research to develop high conviction, while leveraging research, investment ideas and recommendations of sector analysts in the team.

Responsibilities of the role:

  • Fund management responsibilities including retail funds and institutional client portfolios, investing in Greater China/Chinese equities.

  • Conduct in-depth fundamental research and analysis on Chinese equities, share sectoral coverage responsibilities with investment teams, to determine investment merits and risks.

  • Participate in internal portfolio review meetings and meetings/livestreams/teach-ins/roadshows with clients and consultants to discuss investment process, review performance & risk of portfolios, review strategy, outlook, active bets and portfolio changes.

  • Work closely with sales to grow Greater China/Chinese equity AUM and pitch for new business. Be mindful of opportunities to develop new products.

  • Work closely with Asia ex Japan investment team and with our JV partner, Invesco Great Wall, to leverage research and discover new opportunities in China.

Requirement for the Role:

The ideal candidate should be able to clearly articulate a robust investment philosophy and process. Proven track record of at least 3-5 years investing in Greater China/Chinese equities (including China A-shares), and is expected to be an effective communicator who, with the support of our Client Portfolio Managers, can build/strengthen relationships of trust with key internal and external stakeholders, including local/overseas institutional investors, consultants and distributors.

  • Bachelor’s degree holder in Finance / Accounting / Economics or related discipline.

  • 10+ years of relevant work experience in being an analyst and portfolio manager of Greater China/Chinese equities (onshore and offshore).

  • Proven strong performance track record in stock picking, portfolio construction and generating strong risk-adjusted performance for Greater China/Chinese equity funds.

  • Strong writing and communications skills in English and Mandarin Chinese a must.

  • Strong interpersonal skills with proven ability to build trust and relationships.

  • Considers the ultimate impact of decisions and actions on internal and external customers.

  • Mission Driven: care deeply about the impact, goals, and overall values of the firm.

jobs.description:

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for ourclients. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

Job Description

Your Team
You will join a global team of 27+ experienced portfolio managers from diverse backgrounds managing a large number of Equity and Fixed Income ETFs. The newly established Taiwan ETF PM team will play an integral part in managing and growing the local ETF business.

About the Role:

  • Manage passive ETFs, index funds, and feeder ETFs in equity, fixed income, or balanced/multi-asset categories.
  • Ensure compliance with Taiwan regulatory requirements and internal investment processes.
  • Oversee index reconstitution, portfolio trading, cash management, and corporate action decisions.
  • Monitor tracking error and implement strategies to optimize portfolio performance.
  • Collaborate with equity and fixed income analysts and traders to implement portfolio decisions.
  • Lead or contribute to investment process improvements to enhance efficiency and scalability.
  • Engage with internal stakeholders (capital markets, product development) and external clients (institutional due diligence, conferences).
  • Utilize advanced portfolio analytics and modeling tools (e.g., Aladdin, Bloomberg, FactSet, Barra/Axioma) for portfolio optimization.
  • Maintain thorough documentation of policies, procedures, and investment decisions.

Requirement for the Role :

  • Master’s degree in Business, Finance, Economics, or related field preferred.
  • CFA designation or progress towards CFA is preferred but not mandatory.
  • 5 to 15 years of asset management experience, specifically managing passive ETFs or mutual funds in both equity and fixed income.
  • Minimum 5 years of direct experience managing or co-managing passive funds/ETFs.
  • Strong knowledge of global equity and fixed income markets, index construction, and passive investment strategies.
  • Experience with portfolio optimization, tracking error management, and corporate action analysis.
  • Excellent communication skills for client engagement and cross-team collaboration.
  • Experience with portfolio management systems such as Aladdin, Bloomberg, or Charles River is preferred.
  • Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, SQL/Oracle, and VBA is a significant advantage.

  • On-site presence in Taipei office is mandatory.

  • Ability to work under regulatory requirements, personally implementing investment processes.

    Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

  • Ability to work effectively in a team environment with portfolio managers, analysts, traders, compliance, and operations.

    Speak fluent English to communicate with the global team

Taiwan Licensing and Certification Requirements

The candidate must meet one of the following certification and experience criteria:

  • Certified Securities Investment Analyst (CSIA) License
  • Passed a Senior Securities Specialist Examination
  • Passed examination for Securities Investment Trust and Consulting Professionals

jobs.description:

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. Job Description

About the Team / Department:
The ETF Product Strategy Group within the Americas Product is committed to providing superior client support and actionable investment insights that drive our product line’s strategic agenda for growth.

About the Role:

This individual will work closely with others on the team and help develop ETF research and analysis that positions our US ETFs in the current macro environment. The Sr. Analyst will work cross-functionally with Product Development, Portfolio Management, Capital Markets, Sales and Marketing to position and grow the product line.

Responsibilities of the Role:

  • Work alongside analysts and strategists on the team to develop product segment research and analysis on Invesco ETFs, the ETF industry, and broader financial markets serving as a subject matter expert for the product line
  • Develop and update product materials that position Invesco’s ETFs
  • Project manage and lead research projects as assigned by senior leaders
  • Execute on actions needed to maintain and improve product line
  • Partner closely with ETF strategists and specialists to support our clients and ensure product integrity, occasionally participating in client meetings

Requirements of the Role:

  • Undergraduate degree in business, finance, mathematics or related discipline required
  • 3-5 years of investment management and/or research experience preferred
  • Series 7 & 63 (already licensed is a plus, can study for these exams after being hired)
  • CFA track or progress towards advanced strongly preferred
  • Skills and professional development in a senior analyst role with a desire to take on more responsibility and accountability
  • Previous experience or solid understanding of factor, thematic or fixed-income investing strongly preferred
  • ETF and index familiarity and industry experience required
  • Experience with Bloomberg, Morningstar Direct, FactSet, Python a plus
  • Strong research and analytical skills to analyze and interpret various market research reports and data and apply such information to daily activities
  • Strong subject matter acumen and written communication skills
  • Team player with excellent interpersonal skills; maintain effective, cooperative working relationships with all departments and organizational levels
  • Self-directed; must be comfortable with establishing objectives, then working independently to achieve them, seeking the team’s input as needed

The total compensation offered for this position, which includes salary and incentive pay, will vary based on skills, experience and location. The salary range for this position in Downers Grove is $110k to $135k annually.
