jobs.title: Senior Manager, Valuation
jobs.description:
The opportunity

The Senior Manager, Valuation will be responsible for the execution of OTPP’s semi-annual private investment valuation processes, for the purpose of OTPP’s mid-year and year-end financial statement reporting. Private portfolio investments asset classes include private equity, infrastructure, natural resources, venture capital, real estate, and private credit. This position will also actively involve training and reviewing the work of junior staff.

Who you'll work with

The Senior Manager will report to the Director, Valuation.

What you'll do

Leading the planning and execution of the mid-year and year-end valuation processes during peak periods, including:

  • Prepare and oversee the review of “fair value” valuations prepared by OTPP’s Investment teams including the preparation of analysis and summary memorandums in respect thereof

  • Developing an appropriate understanding of the assigned investments and remaining current on individual investment developments through participation in Investment team portfolio monitoring meetings

  • Review the fair value valuations prepared by external valuation agents

  • Develop recommendations of fair value to be used for the purposes of OTPP’s financial statements

  • Support the Director, Valuation in the development, tracking and reporting of plans and timelines to ensure an effective and efficient valuation process

  • Develop the standards for reviews and the corresponding processes to be followed by the staff involved in the valuation process

  • Respond to queries/information requests from OTPP’s external auditors with respect to the private investment valuations

  • Communicate noted issues and generate solutions with respect to the valuation process or specific investment valuations to the Director, Valuation on a timely basis

Interface with the Investment Division with respect to the valuations, including:

  • Coordinating processes and deliverables to facilitate the execution of a smooth mid-year and year-end valuation process

  • Communicating noted issues with respect to the process and/or issues identified with respect to specific investment valuations

  • Ensuring exceptional service is provided to the Investment Division with respect to private investment valuations 

  • Responding to inquiries and requests from the Investment Division including consultation on new transactions where required

Managing external advisor involvement in the mid-year and year-end valuation process, including:

  • Develop recommendations on the nature and use of external advisors including the selection of specific advisors and the scope of their review

  • Managing the entire process around the use of external advisors including:

  • Execution of terms of engagement with specific advisors

  • Establishing timelines, deliverables and reporting processes for external advisors

  • Ensuring internal information required by external advisors is provided by both the Investment Division and Finance in accordance with established timelines

  • Monitoring and reporting on progress of external advisor deliverables and associated costs

  • Review and incorporation of external advisor recommendations/findings into OTPP’s recommendations on fair value

  • Communicating noted issues, and generating solutions, to the Director, Valuation on a timely basis

  • Ensuring exceptional service is provided by external parties

Training, coaching and Supervision of junior staff, including:

  • Be involved in the hiring of junior staff

  • Develop training material for junior staff

  • Provide on the job coaching and train junior staff with respect to valuation standards, practices, and internal process requirements to ensure the execution of effective and efficient valuation processes

Ensure OTPP’s valuation policies and practices remain best practice, including:

  • Review and monitoring of external best practice valuation standards and practices

  • Be involved in the annual review and update of OTPP’s valuation policies including recommending changes based on changes in best practice valuation guidelines and practices

  • Prepare any required OTPP Board reporting with respect to any recommended process or policy changes

  • Contribute to OTPP Board reporting with respect to the results of the mid-year and year end private investment valuation processes

  • Contribute to ad-hoc projects supporting the execution of the broader Finance division strategy

What you'll need

  • Relevant university degree

  • A professional accounting designation (“CPA”), a Chartered Financial Analyst (“CFA”) and/or Chartered Business Valuator (“CBV”) designation

  • Valuation Knowledge – This position requires a solid understanding and application of the principles and complexities of private asset valuation. This includes experience in the application of various valuation approaches as it might relate to private equity, infrastructure, venture capital, natural resources, real estate, private debt, and other private investments.

  • Analytical Thinking and Financial Modelling Skills – Strong analytical thinking, variance analysis, and financial/cash flow modelling skills are important to the execution of the valuation review process.  Having a comfort level in reviewing large and complex financial models is important. 

  • Project Management Skills – OTPP’s valuation process involves the completion of an extensive number of valuation reviews and coordination across multiple departments and teams including within Finance.  Effective planning and project management skills to set priorities, coordinate tasks, supervise junior staff and meet deadlines are essential.

  • Written Communication Skills – This position requires the preparation of written memoranda supported by in-depth valuation analyses.  Strong written communication skills are required to ensure Finance’s memoranda are written clearly and concisely, address the key valuation risks identified and adequately support Finance’s recommendation of fair value.

  • Verbal Communication Skills – This position requires interaction with internal management and various OTPP Investment Division teams for the purpose of completing the valuation review. The nature of the relationship with these parties requires a high degree of professionalism.

  • Experience in private investment valuations (minimum 8 years)

  • Previous experience in coaching, training and developing staff

  • Experience interacting with senior management, external advisors and external auditors is critical

What we’re offering

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage

  • Retirement income through a defined benefit pension plan

  • The opportunity to invest back into the fund through our Deferred Incentive Program

  • A flexible work environment combining in office collaboration and remote working

  • Competitive time off

  • Our Flexible Travel Program gives you the option to work abroad in another region/country for up to a month each year

  • Employee discount programs including Edvantage and Perkopolis

jobs.title: Investment Associate, Teachers' Venture Growth
jobs.description:
Investment Associate, Teachers' Venture Growth

Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan(“Teachers”) is one of Canada’s largest institutional investors. Established as an independent organization in 1990, Teachers’ has built an internationally recognized reputation for innovation and leadership in active investment management. Today, Teachers’ manages more than C$260B in capital on behalf of 340K teachers in the province of Ontario, Canada. Notably, 80% of Teachers’ AUM is managed directly by more than 400 investment professionals operating from offices based in Toronto, San Francisco, London, Singapore and Mumbai.

Teachers’ Venture Growth (“TVG”)is the investment department within OTPP focused on late-stage venture and growth equity investments in disruptive and innovative businesses within the tech ecosystem. Established in 2019, TVG has grown its global investment portfolio to C$10.0B having invested in some of the leading companies in the AI, Cyber, Fintech, and Software sectors. Our global portfolio includes category leaders such as Anthropocene, Databricks, Wayze, Kraken, Quantexa, DeepL, Alan, ComplyAdvantage, Lendable, and Taxfix. TVG’s strategy involves both direct and fund investments and operates with a global team of over 30 professionals.

TVG would typically lead financing rounds and take an active role in closely supporting its portfolio companies, taking board seats, and providing an array of added-value services.

The Opportunity

TVG is looking for an Associate to join the EMEA team, based in London. As a key member of the team, you will contribute to the development and implementation of TVG’s ambitious Europeandirect investment strategy, while gaining exposure to global deal flow.

The Opportunity

TVG is looking for an Associate to join the EMEA team, based in London. As a key member of the team, you will contribute to the development and implementation of TVG’s ambitious European direct investment strategy, while gaining exposure to global deal flow.

What You’ll Do

  • Support origination, due diligence, and execution of investment opportunities across a wide range of sectors within the tech ecosystem in EMEA
  • Perform financial modelling and analysis (financial projections, unit economics analysis, sensitivity analysis, valuations, and returns)
  • Support investment recommendations based on the results of the team’s due diligence findings and analysis
  • Prepare and present investment recommendations and memos to internal committees
  • Evaluate and monitor the ongoing performance of portfolio company investments
  • Proactively add value and build relationships with portfolio companies
  • Establish and build strong relationships within the venture capital ecosystem, including with start-ups, venture funds, corporate partners, and other key stakeholders. You will be able to work and interact with entrepreneurs and executive teams from some of the best high growth technology companies
  • Work cross-functionally and collaboratively with other TVG teams across North America and Asia to ensure continued alignment with Ontario Teachers’ mission, vision, and values

What you’ll Bring

  • Strong academic credentials with a bachelors degree or other relevance academic background
  • 2-3 years of relevant industry experience (preferably in investment banking, venture capital, private equity, or growth equity)
  • Excellent analytical and quantitative skills and ability to perform in-depth analysis
  • Exceptionally curious and driven, with the ability to balance competing priorities, work independently and thrive in a high performance environment
  • Strong work ethic, team mindset, communication and presentation skills
  • Team player with strong interpersonal and relationship building skills, able to build partnerships internally and externally
  • A risk taker and comfortable with ambiguity but able to balance increased risk taking with strategic and operational judgement
  • Demonstrate ability to think independently
  • High proficient in Excel and PowerPoint
  • Proficiency in English is required, other European languages are highly preferred

jobs.title: Director, Hedge Funds
jobs.description:
The Opportunity

We are seeking an experienced investment leader to join our Hedge Funds & ILS (Insurance‑Linked Securities) team as a Director, Hedge Funds. This role offers a compelling opportunity to make an immediate impact on a large, sophisticated hedge fund platform while helping shape its continued evolution as market environments and opportunity sets change.

The Hedge Funds & ILS team manages over $11 billion in capital allocated to external managers across a broad range of strategies and global markets. As part of the Capital Markets department, the team plays a critical role in generating enhanced, diversified returns by partnering with external managers who bring differentiated expertise that complements Ontario Teachers’ internal investment capabilities.

This role is ideal for a senior investor with deep experience in Rates and Macro strategies, strong risk‑taking judgment, and the ability to lead portfolio decisions in dynamic and uncertain market conditions.

Who You’ll Work With

The Hedge Funds & ILS team consists of approximately 10 investment professionals based in Toronto and London, with a mandate to deliver absolute, uncorrelated returns that enhance diversification across the broader portfolio.

You will report directly to the Managing Director, Hedge Funds & ILS, and work within a collaborative decision‑making framework governing portfolio construction, capital allocation, and risk management. As part of a dynamic trading‑floor environment, you will interact daily with colleagues across Capital Markets, leveraging the expertise of more than 100 investment professionals across asset classes and regions.

What You’ll Do

  • Play a key role in the active management of the total hedge fund portfolio, with primary responsibility for strategic thinking and positioning within Rates and Macro strategies.

  • Be directly accountable for the performance and risk management of a portfolio of external hedge fund managers across strategies including Rates Relative Value, Macro, and Multi‑Strategy.

  • Build and leverage your network of relationships to lead the origination of new managers that are accretive to the portfolio

  • Develop and maintain a forward‑looking view of market opportunity sets, adjusting portfolio positioning as market environments evolve.

  • Lead comprehensive investment and operational due diligence on external hedge fund managers, including negotiation of investment terms and coordination with internal partners across legal, compliance, risk, and operations.

  • Monitor portfolio exposures, risk metrics, and P&L across individual managers, strategies, and the overall hedge fund portfolio.

  • Contribute to the development of analytical tools and frameworks that support portfolio construction, risk analysis, and tactical investment decisions.

  • Provide mentorship, guidance, and performance feedback to junior team members, fostering a collaborative and high‑performing investment culture.

  • Contribute to the long‑term vision and strategic direction of the Hedge Funds & ILS platform.

  • Build effective partnerships across Capital Markets and the broader organization to advance shared investment objectives.

What You’ll Bring

  • A minimum of 8 years of relevant experience within an investment bank, asset manager, hedge fund, or fund‑of‑funds environment, ideally with direct exposure to portfolio management and risk taking.

  • Deep expertise in Rates / Fixed Income and Macro strategies, both as asset classes and investment approaches.

  • Proven ability to make risk‑adjusted investment decisions under uncertainty, balancing opportunity, precision, and timing.

  • Broad understanding of the hedge fund landscape, including hedge fund strategies, portfolio theory, valuation, and risk management.

  • Demonstrated investment track record with prior experience leading and developing junior staff.

  • Strong quantitative, analytical, and modeling capabilities to assess risk and return drivers.

  • Undergraduate or postgraduate degree in a relevant discipline such as economics, finance, business, engineering, or computer science, or equivalent experience

  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to convey complex investment concepts to diverse audiences.

  • Strong negotiation and relationship‑management skills.

  • Willingness to travel globally as required.

What we’re offering

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage

  • Retirement income through a defined benefit pension plan

  • The opportunity to invest back into the fund through our Deferred Incentive Program

  • A flexible work environment combining in office collaboration and remote working

  • Competitive time off

  • Our Flexible Travel Program gives you the option to work abroad in another region/country for up to a month each year

  • Employee discount programs including Edvantage and Perkopolis

jobs.title: Investment Associate, Sustainable Investing - Climate and Energy Transition
jobs.description:
The opportunity

The Associate, Sustainable Investing (Climate and Energy Transition) is an entrepreneurial and analytically driven professional who is passionate about the opportunity to help shape the low-carbon transition at scale. This role sits at the intersection of strategy, data, and real-world execution, they will work directly with portfolio companies and internal partners to accelerate decarbonization, strengthen climate resilience, and support long-term value creation for the fund.

As a core member of one of Canada’s leading Sustainable Investing teams, the Associate will contribute to fund-level climate initiatives while engaging deeply with portfolio companies to design and advance credible transition and decarbonization plans. The Associate will collaborate across teams to assess systemic climate risks and concentrations, support ESG due diligence for private investments, contribute research and analytics to stewardship and climate strategies, and help mobilize fund-wide climate programs.

This is a highly impactful opportunity for a professional who combines quantitative rigor, intellectual curiosity, and strong interpersonal skills—and who is eager to help drive meaningful progress in the global low-carbon transition

Who you’ll work with

  • The Sustainable Investing (SI) team has 15 members and works across the organization to ensure that the Fund is effectively managing sustainability related risks and opportunities across the investment lifecycle

  • The Associate will report to the Director, Climate and Energy Transition, who is primarily responsible for implementing our work with private investments across all sectors, including Real Estate, Infrastructure, Natural Resources, Private Equity, and Teachers’ Venture Growth, to evaluate and implement climate transition plans and strategies

  • Given the cross-departmental nature of the Sustainable Investing team, the Associate will interact on a frequent basis with colleagues from various divisions and teams across the fund, especially the private investment departments

What you’ll do

  • Work with other SI team members on a range of fund-level climate change initiatives.

  • Work with portfolio companies to establish transition and/or decarbonization plans – will include evaluating and identifying operational programs or capital project opportunities, contributing to capital planning processes and discussions, providing support for business cases, and supporting implementation as needed

  • Provide expertise in emissions management and monitoring

  • Keep abreast of developments in the climate change space, including policy and regulatory changes, evolving GHG accounting standards, and industry initiatives

  • Support ESG due diligence for private deals – desktop research, review of consultant reports, participation in meetings with deal teams and consultants

  • Support cross–asset class analysis of systemic risk drivers and concentrations using top-down and bottom-up approaches, including the application of climate risk tools and environmental data to assess physical and transition risks

  • Collaborate with internal stakeholders in the execution of fund-wide climate change programs and initiatives

  • Provide research, analytics and communication materials to support any of the evolving climate change, stewardship and social strategies across SI

  • Support other related initiatives as required

What you’ll need

  • Minimum Bachelor; Masters degree in engineering, environment, climate science, finance or other relevant field an asset

  • Completion of the CFA (or underway) would be an asset but not required

  • Knowledge of and passion for sustainable investing, the energy transition and addressing climate change

  • Experience working on decarbonization plans

  • Quantitative modelling skillset (excel, python, matlab, etc.) an asset

  • Strong interpersonal, communication and presentation skills (both oral and written) – particularly an ability to understand and communicate technical climate and sustainability material to investment professionals and company management

  • Familiarity with investment-related concepts, such as valuation models, capital markets and fundamental company analysis an asset

  • Works well independently or as a member of a team

  • Highly adaptable, flexible and comfortable with ambiguity

  • Ability to manage multiple and changing priorities

  • 2-5 years related experience in energy, decarbonization, sustainability, or climate consulting. Direct experience in asset management or value creation with climate change/decarbonization focus an asset.

What we’re offering

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage

  • Retirement income through a defined benefit pension plan

  • The opportunity to invest back into the fund through our Deferred Incentive Program

  • A flexible work environment combining in office collaboration and remote working

  • Competitive time off

  • Our Flexible Travel Program gives you the option to work abroad in another region/country for up to a month each year

  • Employee discount programs including Edvantage and Perkopolis

jobs.title: Managing Director, Private Equity - Software
jobs.description:
The opportunity

The Managing Director will be working with investment professionals with deep sector and regional expertise and will play a key role in making and managing direct investments in the Technology sector.

Who you'll will work with 

Reporting to the Senior Managing Director (SMD), the Managing Director will lead deal teams comprising analysts, associates and principals, supported by third party consultants, operating executives, legal and accounting professionals dedicated to each particular investment.

The individual will have significant prior expertise and a strong track record investing in Technology, particularly in Software with knowledge of emerging AI and digitization themes.  Key focus areas include Application software, Infrastructure software, Digital & IT Services and other Tech-enabled categories.

In certain instances, the Managing Director may also work with colleagues in the London, Singapore or Mumbai offices in evaluating international investment opportunities in a given sector.

What you'll do

  • When evaluating new investments, the Managing Director will lead, with oversight of SMD, a small team determining whether the company represents an attractive investment opportunity. Among other things, the analysis of investments includes conducting industry and company due diligence, creating sophisticated financial models, arranging debt financing, negotiating legal documents, formulating value creation plans (in partnership with operating teams and other lead investors) and preparing written recommendations.

  • Once a transaction has been completed, the Managing Director will remain involved with the business, and will work with our investment partners and the management team to implement value-enhancing initiatives. The Managing Director may be expected to take lead roles at the board and/or sub-committees of the board in some cases. 

  • They will play an active role in origination, identifying target investments and creating a “right to win”, which should include unique value creation levers.  Activities will include conducting desk research, working with consultants, operating executives, attending conferences and trade shows, networking with management teams and meeting with various industry participants. This will require the individual to build high-impact relationships and networks across the Technology investing ecosystem.

  • They will lead sub-sector research and deep dives as the team evolves its coverage strategy and focus areas over time with shifting industry dynamics such as the emergence of AI impacting investment decisions.

  • Collaborate with other Global Sector teams and across Investments on specific departmental initiatives.

  • Seek to lead deals with input and high level of communication from global sector SMD.

What you'll need

  • 15+ years relevant work experience, ideally the most recent experience leading deals in Technology private equity.  Active Technology M&A in a lead role in corporate development would also be considered. Supplemented by previous experiences in management consulting, investment banking and/or corporations (preferably corporate development or similar roles).

  • Tenured private equity investor with a strong track record (technology/software experience strongly preferred). Please include deal sheets with your application.

  • Experience in playing a leading role in the investment process, including conducting due diligence, leading multiple diligence streams such as financial, operational, legal, tax, insurance as well as arranging debt financing and negotiating documentation.

  • Significant experience serving on Boards of Directors

  • Established network of industry professionals, including mid to bulge bracket private equity, senior executives, and advisors.

  • Experience in playing a leading role in the investment process, including conducting due diligence, arranging debt financing and negotiating documentation.

  • Strong negotiation, collaboration and communication skills.

  • An ability to balance priorities, work independently, lead projects and thrive in a high performance team environment.

  • Willingness and ability to travel globally.

jobs.title: Life Actuarial Valuation, Reporting, and Forecasting Actuary
jobs.description:
Why USAA?

At USAA, our mission is to empower our members to achieve financial security through highly competitive products, exceptional service and trusted advice. We seek to be the #1 choice for the military community and their families.

Embrace a fulfilling career at USAA, where our core values – honesty, integrity, loyalty and service – define how we treat each other and our members. Be part of what truly makes us special and impactful.

The Opportunity

As a dedicated Life Actuarial Valuation, Reporting, and Forecasting Actuary, you will be responsible for Life or Annuity reserving, reporting, or forecasting. They will be part of a critical team working closely with both the Life Company and Finance teams.

Coordinates, prepares, and performs actuarial analyses required to develop new products, project future results, reprice existing products, and/or determine financial strength.

We offer a flexible work environment that requires an individual to be in the office 4 days per week. This position can be based in one of the following locations: San Antonio or Charlotte, NC.

Relocation assistance is not available for this position.

What you'll do:

  • Performs complex work assignments utilizing actuarial modeling software driven models for pricing, valuation, and/or risk management.
  • Reviews laws and regulations to ensure all processes are compliant; and provides recommendations for improvements and monitors industry communications regarding potential changes to existing laws and regulations.
  • Runs models, generates reports, and presents recommendations and detailed analysis of all model runs to Actuarial Leadership.
  • May make recommendations for model adjustments and improvements, when appropriate.
  • Shares knowledge with team members and serves as a resource to team on escalated issues and navigates obstacles to deliver work product.
  • Leads or participates as a key resource on moderately complex projects through concept, planning, execution, and implementation phases with minimal guidance, involving cross functional actuarial areas.
  • Develops exhibits and reports that help explain proposals/findings and provides information in an understandable and usable format for stakeholders.
  • Identifies and provides recommended solutions to business problems independently, often presenting recommendation to leadership.
  • Maintains proper price level, price structure, data availability and other requirements to achieve profitability and competitive goals.
  • Identifies critical assumptions to monitor and suggest timely remedies to correct or prevent unfavorable trends.
  • Tests impact of assumptions by identifying sources of gain and loss, the appropriate premiums, interest margins, reserves, and cash values for profitability and viability of new and existing products.
  • Advises management on issues and serves as a primary resource for their individual team members on escalated issues.
  • Ensures risks associated with business activities are effectively identified, measured, monitored, and controlled in accordance with risk and compliance policies and procedures.

What you have:

  • Bachelor's degree; OR 4 years of related experience (in addition to the minimum years of experience required) may be substituted in lieu of degree.
  • 4 years relevant actuarial or analytical experience and attainment of Fellow within the Society of Actuaries; OR 8 years relevant actuarial experience and attainment of Associate within the Society of Actuaries.
  • Experience performing complex work assignments utilizing actuarial modeling software driven models for pricing, valuation, and/or risk management.
  • Demonstrated experience communicating complex actuarial analysis and recommendations to technical and non-technical audiences.

What sets you apart:

  • 5+ years of Moody’s AXIS experience
  • Advanced degree
  • US military experience through military service or a military spouse/domestic partner

Compensation range: The salary range for this position is: $127,310-$243,340

USAA does not provide visa sponsorship for this role. Please do not apply for this role if at any time (now or in the future) you will need immigration support (i.e., H-1B, TN, STEM OPT Training Plans, etc.).

Compensation: USAA has an effective process for assessing market data and establishing ranges to ensure we remain competitive. You are paid within the salary range based on your experience and market data of the position. The actual salary for this role may vary by location.

Employees may be eligible for pay incentives based on overall corporate and individual performance and at the discretion of the USAA Board of Directors.

The above description reflects the details considered necessary to describe the principal functions of the job and should not be construed as a detailed description of all the work requirements that may be performed in the job.

Benefits: At USAA our employees enjoy best-in-class benefits to support their physical, financial, and emotional wellness. These benefits include comprehensive medical, dental and vision plans, 401(k), pension, life insurance, parental benefits, adoption assistance, paid time off program with paid holidays plus 16 paid volunteer hours, and various wellness programs. Additionally, our career path planning and continuing education assists employees with their professional goals.

For more details on our outstanding benefits, visit our benefits page on USAAjobs.com.

Applications for this position are accepted on an ongoing basis, this posting will remain open until the position is filled. Thus, interested candidates are encouraged to apply the same day they view this posting.

USAA is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or status as a protected veteran.

Founded in 2005 and based in New York, the firm manages capital for institutional investors globally. CIFC focuses primarily on leveraged finance markets, including collateralized loan obligations and opportunistic credit. It is known for its rigorous fundamental credit analysis, active management approach, and commitment to generating alpha. The firm leverages deep research capabilities and a disciplined investment process to identify value across the credit spectrum.

Opening: 2026-02-24  |  Closing: Rolling
